hf:2603.12255.task: Streaming visual-based spatial intelligence from long-horizon video
hf:2603.12255.key_results: State-of-the-art performance on video spatial benchmarks with efficient long-horizon scene understanding
hf:2603.12255.comments: Test-time training approach with 3D spatiotemporal convolution is computationally interesting but scalability to truly unbounded streams remains unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Spatial-predictive TTT mechanism with 3D spatiotemporal convolution captures geometric correspondence across frames
hypotheses.result: Improved long-horizon spatial understanding demonstrated on benchmarks

hf:2603.12180.task: Evaluating strategic reasoning vs. stochastic search in document-intensive agent workflows
hf:2603.12180.key_results: Best agents match human accuracy but succeed on different questions; ~20% gap to oracle; agents rely on brute-force search
hf:2603.12180.comments: Novel accuracy-effort trade-off metric is valuable, but the finding that agents use brute-force despite capability suggests fundamental limitations in current agentic planning.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Agents demonstrate genuine strategic reasoning for complex document retrieval
hypotheses.result: Agents rely on brute-force search, fail to close 20% gap to oracle performance

hf:2603.12201.task: Accelerating sparse attention via cross-layer index reuse in LLMs
hf:2603.12201.key_results: 1.82× prefill speedup, 1.48× decode speedup; 75% indexer computation reduction with negligible quality loss
hf:2603.12201.comments: Clever exploitation of cross-layer redundancy in sparse attention; validates that top-k selections are highly similar across consecutive layers.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Top-k sparse attention indices are redundant across consecutive layers
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; partitioning into Full/Shared layers removes 75% of indexer computation

hf:2603.10178.task: Video-based reward modeling for computer-use agent evaluation
hf:2603.10178.key_results: 84.7% accuracy, 87.7% recall; outperforms GPT-4o and Gemini-3 Pro on multi-platform tasks
hf:2603.10178.comments: Spatiotemporal token pruning is elegant for handling long high-resolution video; model-agnostic evaluation framework is valuable for agent research.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Video-execution reward modeling provides scalable, model-agnostic evaluation for computer-use agents
hypotheses.result: ExeVRM achieves SOTA accuracy/recall, precise temporal attribution across multiple platforms

hf:2603.11421.task: Text-driven multi-shot cinematic video generation with camera control
hf:2603.11421.key_results: Superior cinematic aesthetics; camera-accurate and cross-shot consistent multi-shot video generation
hf:2603.11421.comments: Plan-then-Control decoupling with VLM-based planner is sensible; automated multi-shot calibration pipeline addresses a real bottleneck in film creation.

model_io.model: ShotVerse (VLM Planner + Controller with camera adapter)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Aligned (Caption, Trajectory, Video) triplets form joint distribution enabling precise cinematic control
hypotheses.result: Data-centric approach bridges gap between unreliable text control and manual plotting

hf:2603.12257.task: Multi-subject identity-preserving video customization with omni-motion control
hf:2603.12257.key_results: Precise multi-subject identity control; robust omni-motion control (global, local, camera); high-quality videos with motion-aware identity preservation
hf:2603.12257.comments: Two-stage paradigm with latent identity reward learning is innovative; group/role embeddings effectively disentangle multi-subject scenes.

model_io.model: DreamVideo-Omni (diffusion backbone + latent identity reward model)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Latent identity reward feedback learning preserves identity during motion-controlled generation
hypotheses.result: Mitigates identity degradation; motion-aware rewards align with human preferences

hf:2603.12056.task: Continual learning from experience and skills in multimodal agents
hf:2603.12056.key_results: Consistent improvements on five benchmarks across diverse domains; superior zero-shot generalization vs. tool-only and learning baselines
hf:2603.12056.comments: Dual-stream (experience + skills) framework is well-motivated; visual grounding of knowledge extraction/retrieval is practical for embodied agents.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Experiences and skills are complementary forms of knowledge for agent improvement
hypotheses.result: Both knowledge streams play distinct roles in influencing reasoning; show complementary effects

hf:2603.12250.task: Deterministic video depth estimation with generative priors
hf:2603.12250.key_results: SOTA zero-shot depth performance; uses 163× less task-specific data than leading baselines; seamless long-video inference
hf:2603.12250.comments: Novel use of diffusion timestep as structural anchor and latent manifold rectification is clever; breaking the generative-discriminative trade-off is meaningful.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Diffusion models contain profound geometric priors for depth despite being trained generatively
hypotheses.result: Deterministic adaptation achieves SOTA zero-shot performance with minimal task-specific data

hf:2603.11593.task: Text-centric image editing with precise glyph manipulation
hf:2603.11593.key_results: Outperforms previous open-source models; 330K training pairs across 15 languages; handles multilingual text editing
hf:2603.11593.comments: HTML-based automatic pipeline is scalable; glyph-guided SFT + multi-objective RL addresses real failure modes in text editing.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicit spatial/content priors via glyph guidance improve text editing precision
hypotheses.result: Glyph-guided training reduces blurry/hallucinated characters; RL alignment improves instruction adherence

hf:2603.12247.task: Robust reward modeling for faithful image editing and generation via RL
hf:2603.12247.key_results: SOTA human alignment; mitigates hallucinations; substantial performance breakthroughs in editing and generation fidelity
hf:2603.12247.comments: Comprehensive framework with high-quality datasets (FIRM-Edit-370K, FIRM-Gen-293K) and Base-and-Bonus reward strategy is well-designed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Task-specific reward models with high-quality curation align better with human judgment than generic metrics
hypotheses.result: FIRM achieves superior alignment; Base-and-Bonus strategy balances competing objectives effectively

hf:2603.12245.task: Decoupling compute from image resolution in diffusion transformers via elastic latent interfaces
hf:2603.12245.key_results: 35.3% FID gain, 39.6% FDD gain on ImageNet-1K 512px; dynamic latent count enables latency-quality trade-offs
hf:2603.12245.comments: ELIT's minimal design (two cross-attention layers) is elegant; importance-ordered latent representations enable principled compute budgeting.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Variable-length latent sequences enable compute-constrained generation without retraining
hypotheses.result: Training with random latent dropping produces importance-ordered representations; FID/FDD gains confirmed

hf:2603.09160.task: Dense image captioning via rubric-guided reinforcement learning
hf:2603.09160.key_results: Highest win rates on CapArena; 7B model matches Qwen2.5-VL-32B; 3B model surpasses its 7B counterpart
hf:2603.09160.comments: LLM-written rubrics for fine-grained reward signals is creative; structured multi-faceted evaluation avoids coarse scalar rewards.

model_io.model: RubiCap (RL framework with LLM rubric writer and judge)

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLM-derived rubrics provide better reward signals than scalar scores for captioning RL
hypotheses.result: RubiCap outperforms supervised distillation and prior RL methods; achieves strong benchmarks

hf:2603.12264.task: Benchmarking discipline-informed reasoning in image editing
hf:2603.12264.key_results: 520 samples across 10 academic domains; reveals substantial limitations in current models under knowledge-intensive settings
hf:2603.12264.comments: Multi-dimensional evaluation (Discipline Reasoning, Visual Consistency, Logical Readability) is rigorous; exposes key gaps in unified multimodal models.

model_io.model: Various image editing models (20 SOTA open/closed-source)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current models struggle with discipline-specific constraints in image editing
hypotheses.result: Large performance gaps; models lack discipline-informed reasoning under implicit, knowledge-intensive settings

hf:2603.09970.task: Evaluating LLMs' capacity for creative associative reasoning
hf:2603.09970.key_results: Frontier models show varying creative utility; thinking models not always more effective despite high token budgets
hf:2603.09970.comments: CREATE's focus on associative reasoning as a measurable creativity component is novel; large search space enables scaling without saturation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Thinking models improve performance on creative associative reasoning tasks
hypotheses.result: Thinking models not always more effective; recent creative prompting gives limited improvement

hf:2603.12267.task: Adaptive-length video tokenization for efficient autoregressive generation
hf:2603.12267.key_results: 24.4% token savings vs. SOTA; SOTA class-to-video generation on UCF-101; superior reconstruction and generation quality
hf:2603.12267.comments: Adaptive routing based on video complexity is intuitive; semantic encoder integration improves efficiency-quality trade-off.

model_io.model: EVATok (adaptive video tokenizer with router network)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive token assignment improves efficiency over uniform tokenization
hypotheses.result: EVATok achieves 24.4% token savings; superior quality-cost trade-off on reconstruction and generation

hf:2603.12265.task: Unified streaming visual backbone for perception, reconstruction, and action
hf:2603.12265.key_results: Competitive performance across image/video probing, geometric reconstruction, reasoning, and robotic manipulation
hf:2603.12265.comments: Single-backbone approach with causal spatiotemporal attention and 3D-RoPE is elegant; pre-training on 29 datasets enables broad generalization.

model_io.model: OmniStream (streaming visual backbone with causal attention and 3D-RoPE)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Single versatile backbone generalizes across semantic, spatial, and temporal reasoning tasks
hypotheses.result: Competitive performance with frozen backbone across diverse downstream tasks

hf:2603.12252.task: Scaling endogenous chain-of-thought reasoning in diffusion models
hf:2603.12252.key_results: 92.1% average accuracy across benchmarks; 8.3 percentage points above strongest baseline
hf:2603.12252.comments: EndoCoT's iterative refinement of latent thoughts within the diffusion process is clever; bridges MLLM reasoning and DiT denoising.

model_io.model: EndoCoT (diffusion with iterative thought guidance + terminal grounding)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Iterative thought guidance enables DiT to progressively decompose complex instructions
hypotheses.result: Activates MLLM reasoning; achieves SOTA on spatial reasoning, puzzle tasks

hf:2603.12228.task: Discovering task-specific experts via random sampling around pretrained weights
hf:2603.12228.key_results: Competitive with PPO/GRPO/ES for large models; shows task-experts populate substantial fraction of pretrained neighborhood
hf:2603.12228.comments: Perspective on pretraining as a distribution over solutions is insightful; simple ensemble approach is surprisingly effective for large models.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Large pretrained models contain diverse task-experts in neighborhood around pretrained weights
hypotheses.result: Simple random sampling+ensemble competitive with complex post-training methods

hf:2603.10444.task: Stabilizing low-bit FP4 LLM training via mean bias correction
hf:2603.10444.key_results: Substantially narrows loss gap to BF16; restores downstream performance; hardware-efficient stable low-bit training
hf:2603.10444.comments: Identifying rank-one mean bias as primary instability driver is insightful; simple mean-subtraction is practical compared to SVD methods.

model_io.model: FP4 (W4A4G4) quantized LLM training with mean removal

hypotheses.hypothesis: Rank-one mean bias drives dynamic-range inflation in low-precision LLM training
hypotheses.result: Mean removal recovers stability; gap to BF16 substantially narrowed

hf:2603.11531.task: Real-time 3D Gaussian Splatting on mobile devices
hf:2603.11531.key_results: Real-time rendering on edge devices; compact model size; preserves high visual quality
hf:2603.11531.comments: Depth-aware order-independent rendering eliminates expensive sorting; neural view-dependent enhancement handles transparency artifacts elegantly.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Order-independent rendering accelerates Gaussian Splatting without major quality loss
hypotheses.result: Eliminates sorting bottleneck; neural enhancement models view-dependent effects

hf:2603.11327.task: Meta-RL for agentic search with in-context self-reflection
hf:2603.11327.key_results: 9.2-19.3% relative improvements across 8 benchmarks; strong generalization with cross-episode learning
hf:2603.11327.comments: Self-reflection across episodes enables principled in-context meta-learning; multi-turn RL with turn-level credit assignment is technically sound.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cross-episode self-reflection improves in-context exploration at test-time
hypotheses.result: MR-Search outperforms single-episode RL baselines; generalizes across benchmarks

hf:2603.11103.task: Pre-training LLMs via reconstructed agentic trajectories from static code repositories
hf:2603.11103.key_results: Enhances long-context understanding, coding proficiency, and agentic capabilities; continuous pre-training on synthetic trajectories improves downstream performance
hf:2603.11103.comments: Reverse-engineering latent agentic trajectories from code is creative; multi-agent simulation grounded in code structure ensures fidelity.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reconstructed agentic trajectories provide richer supervision than static code repositories
hypotheses.result: Continuous pre-training on synthetic data enhances Llama-3-8B performance

hf:2603.10652.task: Improving robustness of video reasoning models to real-world perturbations
hf:2603.10652.key_results: 24% relative accuracy improvement, 9%+ reasoning improvement on perturbed data; consistent gains on clean benchmarks
hf:2603.10652.comments: ROVA's difficulty-aware online training with robustness-aware consistency rewards is principled; PVRBench adds valuable real-world evaluation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Robustness-aware consistency rewards improve generalization under real-world perturbations
hypotheses.result: ROVA mitigates 35% performance degradation from weather/occlusion/motion

hf:2603.10055.task: Pre-training LLMs via neural cellular automata synthetic data
hf:2603.10055.key_results: 6% improvement on downstream language modeling; 1.6× faster convergence; outperforms 1.6B Common Crawl tokens despite less compute
hf:2603.10055.comments: NCA-generated synthetic data as pre-pre-training is novel; domain-dependent complexity suggests potential for targeted synthetic distributions.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Synthetic NCA data improves pre-training efficiency compared to natural language alone
hypotheses.result: 164M NCA tokens improve downstream modeling; outperforms 1.6B CC tokens; domain-dependent complexity variation observed

hf:2603.12145.task: Automatic high-performance RL environment generation via LLM agents
hf:2603.12145.key_results: 1.5-22320× speedups; low (<$10) synthesis cost; semantic equivalence verified; zero sim-to-sim gap across backends
hf:2603.12145.comments: Agent-assisted repair with hierarchical verification is comprehensive; TCGJax demonstrates generalization to unseen specifications.

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLM agents can reliably generate high-performance semantically-equivalent environments with hierarchical verification
hypotheses.result: 5 environments synthesized with property/interaction/rollout verification; zero sim-to-sim gap confirmed

hf:2603.11510.task: Multilingual language modeling at 3.35B parameters with region-aware specialization
hf:2603.11510.key_results: SOTA translation quality, strong multilingual understanding, high-quality generation across 70 languages with just 3.35B parameters
hf:2603.11510.comments: Region-specialized variants and globally balanced instruction-tuning represent practical efficiency-specialization trade-off.

model_io.model: Tiny Aya (3.35B multilingual LM with region variants)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Region-aware posttraining improves multilingual balance and quality
hypotheses.result: Globally balanced and region-specialized variants achieve SOTA translation quality

hf:2603.10420.task: Industrial-grade all-in-one automatic speech recognition system
hf:2603.10420.key_results: SOTA on benchmarks: ASR 2.89% CER (Mandarin), VAD 97.57% F1, LID 97.18% accuracy (82 langs), Punc 78.90% F1
hf:2603.10420.comments: Unified pipeline with four specialized modules is practical; DFSMN-based VAD is lightweight (0.6M params); comprehensive dialect/accent coverage.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Integrated pipeline with specialized modules achieves better performance than single-model approaches
hypotheses.result: SOTA across all four modalities; practical for production deployment

hf:2603.10365.task: Principled latent design for diffusion models via Vision Foundation Model priors
hf:2603.10365.key_results: gFID 1.82 (80 epochs), 1.31 (800 epochs) on ImageNet-1K 256×256 without CFG; superior compression and stability
hf:2603.10365.comments: Geometric Autoencoder's latent normalization replacing KL-divergence is principled; dynamic noise sampling ensures stability under high-intensity noise.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Optimized latent design from VFM priors improves diffusion learning efficiency
hypotheses.result: GAE achieves SOTA gFID with superior equilibrium between compression, semantics, and stability

hf:2603.12057.task: Coarse-guided visual generation via weighted h-transform sampling
hf:2603.12057.key_results: Effective guidance for diverse image/video generation tasks; maintains quality and guidance adherence
hf:2603.12057.comments: h-transform approach with noise-level-aware scheduling elegantly addresses guidance-quality trade-off without requiring forward operator knowledge.

hypotheses.hypothesis: h-transform guidance balances instruction adherence and synthesis quality without operator knowledge
hypotheses.result: Training-free method generalizes across image and video tasks

hf:2603.11076.task: Scaling diversity in agentic task synthesis for tool-use generalization
hf:2603.11076.key_results: +22 average improvement on OOD benchmarks; diversity scaling outperforms quantity scaling 4× efficiency gain
hf:2603.11076.comments: Evidence-driven task derivation from tool executions ensures grounding; controlled scaling analysis provides actionable insights for dataset construction.

outputs: diverse executable tasks with multi-step tool-use patterns

hypotheses.hypothesis: Diversity scaling outperforms quantity scaling for OOD generalization in tool use
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; diversity axis (tool-pool coverage, toolset variety) more important than task count

hf:2603.07534.task: Controllable accent manipulation in multilingual TTS without accented training data
hf:2603.07534.key_results: Fine-grained accent control; compositional accent mixing; generalizes beyond English to multiple languages
hf:2603.07534.comments: Task vector approach enabling accent control without paired accented data is practical; interpolation enables smooth accent gradation.

model_io.model: Accent Vector (task vector derived from non-English native speech)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Task vectors from native speech of different languages enable accent control in target language
hypotheses.result: Fine-grained and compositional accent control achieved; generalizes across languages

hf:2603.12246.task: Evaluating reasoning judges in non-verifiable LLM post-training
hf:2603.12246.key_results: Reasoning judges better than non-reasoning; non-reasoning suffer reward hacking; reasoning judges learn adversarial outputs for benchmarks
hf:2603.12246.comments: Honest finding: reasoning judges can achieve strong performance via adversarial outputs; highlights both capability and limitation of LLM judges.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reasoning judges provide better guidance than non-reasoning judges for RL training
hypotheses.result: True, but reasoning judges can optimize for benchmark deception rather than genuine improvement

hf:2603.11665.task: Multi-task RL optimization for MLLM-as-a-Judge across diverse visual tasks
hf:2603.11665.key_results: Superior judgment consistency and human preference correlation; robust OOD generalization
hf:2603.11665.comments: Multi-task RL approach for judge generalization is well-motivated; addresses key limitation of single-task judge optimization.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-task RL improves judge generalization across diverse visual tasks
hypotheses.result: MT-RL-Judge outperforms baselines on consistency and OOD generalization

hf:2603.11178.task: Principled distillation at the frontier of student competence
hf:2603.11178.key_results: Significant improvements over base model; Beta-kernel weighting theoretical justification; forward+reverse KL schedule optimal
hf:2603.11178.comments: Theoretical analysis proving SNR vanishing at pass-rate extremes is elegant; paced weighting is simple and effective.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Beta-kernel weighting optimizes signal-to-noise ratio in distillation
hypotheses.result: Proven theoretically; empirical gains on reasoning benchmarks with forward-then-reverse KL

hf:2603.12046.task: Analyzing modality contributions in audio-visual speech recognition via Shapley values
hf:2603.12046.key_results: Models shift toward visual reliance under noise; persistent audio bias despite degradation; modality balance evolves during generation
hf:2603.12046.comments: Shapley-based attribution reveals modality dynamics across SNR levels; identifies actionable bias for ad-hoc correction.

model_io.model: AVSR models with Shapley attribution analysis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Models maintain high audio contributions even under severe noise despite visual reliance strategy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; persistent audio bias observed; SNR is dominant weighting factor

hf:2603.07865.task: Accelerating text-to-audio diffusion via warm-starting from semantically similar cached audio
hf:2603.07865.key_results: 1.8-3.0× latency reduction with ~1K cache; preserves or improves perceptual quality
hf:2603.07865.comments: Training-free, model-agnostic approach with semantic+duration gating is practical; quality-aware cache management maintains utility.

model_io.model: SoundWeaver (reference selector + skip gater + cache manager)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Semantically similar cached audio enables effective warm-starting for diffusion speedup
hypotheses.result: 1.8-3.0× latency reduction with small cache; perceptual quality preserved

hf:2603.12089.task: Traceable black-box watermarking for federated language models
hf:2603.12089.key_results: Near 100% verification rates; robust to removal attacks; negligible primary task impact; client-level traceability
hf:2603.12089.comments: Server-side black-box approach eliminates need for white-box access; unique identity-specific watermarks enable individual culprit attribution.

outputs: watermarked model with client-specific identity markers

hypotheses.hypothesis: Identity-specific watermarks enable traceability of leaked federated models
hypotheses.result: Achieves near 100% verification; robust to fine-tuning, pruning, quantization

hf:2603.11653.task: Continual RL for VLA models with simple sequential fine-tuning
hf:2603.11653.key_results: Seq. FT+LoRA matches or exceeds sophisticated CRL methods; strong plasticity, minimal forgetting, zero-shot generalization
hf:2603.11653.comments: Synergy between pretraining, parameter-efficient adaptation, and on-policy RL reshapes stability-plasticity trade-off; simplicity is strength.

model_io.model: Vision-Language-Action model with LoRA sequential fine-tuning

hypotheses.hypothesis: Simple sequential fine-tuning with LoRA achieves robust continual learning in VLAs
hypotheses.result: Outperforms sophisticated CRL methods; low forgetting via synergy of pretraining+LoRA+on-policy RL

hf:2603.11487.task: Proving necessity of attention sinks in softmax transformers via trigger-conditional tasks
hf:2603.11487.key_results: Attention sinks provably necessary for trigger-conditional behavior; non-normalized ReLU eliminates sinks; normaliza constraint is root cause
hf:2603.11487.comments: Theoretical proof that softmax normalization forces attention collapse to default state is rigorous; ReLU control validates mechanism.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Softmax normalization constraint necessarily induces attention sinks for default state computation
hypotheses.result: Proven theoretically; ReLU attention eliminates sinks, confirming normalization as root cause

hf:2603.11045.task: Differentiable physics-informed 3D reconstruction of material properties from thermal measurements
hf:2603.11045.key_results: Improved subsurface defect localization; enforces thermodynamic laws as hard constraints; efficient 3D tomography
hf:2603.11045.comments: Neural field parameterization with rigorous solver avoids soft-constraint PINN gradient stiffness; discretize-then-optimize is principled.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Differentiable physics solver with neural fields recovers subsurface defects better than PINNs
hypotheses.result: Hard constraints improve accuracy; avoids gradient stiffness in transient diffusion

hf:2603.08258.task: Weight direction-aware distillation for one-step diffusion synthesis
hf:2603.08258.key_results: SOTA FID on COCO; 10% trainable params of baseline; strong generalization to downstream tasks
hf:2603.08258.comments: Analysis revealing weight direction changes > weight norm changes is insightful; LoRaD's low-rank rotation modeling is elegant.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Weight direction changes dominate in diffusion distillation; LoRaD efficiently models them
hypotheses.result: SOTA FID with 10% params; generalizes well to controllable generation and inversion

hf:2603.07988.task: Decentralized multi-agent humanoid control for cooperative human-object interactions
hf:2603.07988.key_results: High success rates across team sizes 2-8; coherent cooperation; single policy for variable team sizes
hf:2603.07988.comments: Transformer-based policy with teammate tokens enables scalable coordination; masked AMP strategy clever use of single-human reference data.

model_io.model: TeamHOI (transformer policy with teammate tokens + masked AMP)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Teammate tokens enable single policy to handle variable team sizes in cooperative HOI
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; masked AMP produces realistic multi-agent cooperation from single-human references

hf:2603.06922.task: Understanding FFN information flow dynamics in LLMs via eigenspectral analysis
hf:2603.06922.key_results: FFN nonlinearities reinject variance across eigenmodes; optimizer geometry modulates variance reinjection; spectral signatures correlate with generalization
hf:2603.06922.comments: NerVE's four complementary metrics comprehensively capture FFN dynamics; insights actionable for architectural/optimizer design.

model_io.model: LLM FFN layers with eigenspectral tracking

hypotheses.hypothesis: FFN nonlinearities govern latent dimension utilization via variance reinjection
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; spectral signatures stable and predictable across scales/architectures; correlate with generalization

hf:2603.05483.task: Comprehensive benchmark for heterogeneous treatment effect estimation in survival analysis
hf:2603.05483.key_results: First rigorous comparison of survival HTE methods; benchmarks synthetic, semi-synthetic, real datasets with ground truth
hf:2603.05483.comments: Modular suite with systematic variation of causal assumptions enables thorough evaluation; addresses fragmented evaluation practices.

model_io.model: Various survival HTE methods (Causal Forests, meta-learners, imputation)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Survival HTE methods degrade under assumption violations and censoring
hypotheses.result: First comprehensive evaluation showing method-specific vulnerabilities to violations

hf:2602.24181.task: Learning modality-agnostic feature space from multi-modal inputs via frozen foundation model anchoring
hf:2602.24181.key_results: Strong cross-modal alignment (RGB-Depth, Segmentation); retains discriminative semantics of frozen teacher
hf:2602.24181.comments: Dual objective (cross-modal alignment + teacher distillation) elegant; addresses real limitation of foundation models like DINOv2.

model_io.model: Omnivorous Vision Encoder (student with cross-modal alignment + distillation)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cross-modal alignment improves feature consistency while distillation preserves semantic quality
hypotheses.result: Student produces consistent embeddings across modalities while retaining DINOv2 quality

hf:2603.10604.task: Real-time photorealism enhancement for synthetic data via lightweight image-to-image translation
hf:2603.10604.key_results: Superior inference latency, visual realism, semantic robustness vs. SOTA paired translation; hybrid training improves quality
hf:2603.10604.comments: Hybrid training with matched patches from real data is practical for domain adaptation; U-Net lightweight design enables real-time.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hybrid training with real-world patches improves visual realism and semantic consistency
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; HyPER-GAN outperforms paired translation baselines in latency and quality

hf:2603.10125.task: 4D equine reconstruction from monocular video via disentangled motion and appearance
hf:2603.10125.key_results: SOTA on real-world APT36K and AiM datasets; generalizes from synthetic training; high-fidelity animatable 3D avatar
hf:2603.10125.comments: Disentanglement into motion+appearance is elegant; masked AMP leverages single-human data; synthetic datasets (VarenPoser, VarenTex) enable scaling.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Disentangling motion and appearance enables efficient 4D reconstruction from monocular video
hypotheses.result: SOTA on real datasets despite synthetic-only training; masked AMP effective for appearance

hf:2603.07331.task: Causal attribution of coastal water clarity degradation to industrial nickel processing expansion
hf:2603.07331.key_results: Bayesian structural time-series establishes causal link; euphotic zone shoaling in oligotrophic waters supporting biodiversity
hf:2603.07331.comments: Satellite-based quasi-experimental causal inference framework is transferable; addresses policy-relevant environmental impact quantification.

inputs: multi-decadal satellite ocean color time series

hypotheses.hypothesis: IMIP nickel processing expansion causally deteriorated nearby coastal water clarity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via BSTS; consensus breakpoint and placebo test validate causal claim

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-69170-0.task: Elucidate role of solvent forces and substrate symmetry on designed protein assembly at liquid-solid interfaces
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-69170-0.key_results: High-speed AFM + machine learning tracked protein nanorod assembly on disparate-symmetry mica substrates; Monte Carlo simulations reproduced observed smectic phase emergence driven by crystal symmetry-induced directional bias; hydration structure imprinting on substrate modulates assembly patterns
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-69170-0.comments: Integration of physics-based simulations with hydration-layer physics reveals important design oversight in current protein-design algorithms; coupling to de novo design platforms is promising but requires further validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Crystal symmetry and hydration structure impart directional bias stabilizing smectic phases
hypotheses.result: Simulations reproduced observed smectic phases; non-interacting rod model alone insufficient

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70409-z.task: Screen and characterize WDR5 oligomerization inducers via nanopore technology
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70409-z.key_results: WZ-1 identified as selective WDR5 oligomerizer; forms intramolecular disulfide at Cys248; triggers thio–disulfide exchange oligomerization; potent anti-tumor activity demonstrated in cells
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70409-z.comments: Nanopore-based screening combined with cryo-EM provides compelling validation of the CaPPO mechanism, though translation to other protein targets remains to be tested.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Disulfide motif in WZ-1 acts as molecular scout to initiate oligomerization
hypotheses.result: Structure-activity relationship confirmed; disulfide necessary for activity

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70514-z.task: Push limit of nanofluidic scattering microscopy to sub-10-kDa label-free biomolecule characterization
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70514-z.key_results: Extended detection range from ≈60 kDa to <6 kDa molecular weight and ≈1.5 nm hydrodynamic radius using ultrasmall nanofluidic channels and hierarchical vision transformer; demonstrated on insulin peptide; approaches Cramér–Rao Lower Bound with sufficient trajectory length
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70514-z.comments: Vision transformer augmentation of standard trajectory analysis is innovative and well-validated against theoretical bounds; applicability to diverse peptide hormones, cytokines, and chemokines promises broad impact.

model_io.model: Nanofluidic scattering microscopy + hierarchical vision transformer analysis
inputs: single biomolecules in subwavelength nanofluidic channels

hypotheses.hypothesis: Vision transformer-augmented trajectory analysis can extend mass/size range to sub-10-kDa regime approaching theoretical limits
hypotheses.result: Characterized insulin (≈5.8 kDa) label-free; approached CRLB precision with sufficient trajectory length

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70575-0.task: Resolve pH-sensing and ivermectin-modulation mechanisms in mite chloride channel SsCl
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70575-0.key_results: Cryo-EM structures at 3.6–3.7 Å in closed (pH 6.5) and desensitized (pH 9) states; desensitized state adopts hourglass conformation; His and Glu residues contribute to pH-sensing network; ivermectin enhances open-state at pH 9 and enables atypical activation at pH 6.5
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70575-0.comments: Hourglass desensitized conformation is structurally unexpected and suggests gating mechanisms differ from cation-selective homologs; functional implications for scabies treatment warrant further study.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Extracellular His and Glu residues form a pH-sensing network similar to cation channels
hypotheses.result: Mutagenesis confirmed impact on pH-sensitivity; network identified via structural analysis

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70643-5.task: Determine high-resolution structure and regulatory mechanisms of GSDME pore assembly
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70643-5.key_results: Cryo-EM structures of 27- and 28-fold GSDME pores at 3.58–3.64 Å; extended transmembrane β-barrel architecture identified; caspase-3 activates via DMPD tetrapeptide recognition in linker; S-palmitoylation at Cys180 enhances pore-forming efficiency
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70643-5.comments: Structure-guided mutagenesis validates lipid-binding and oligomerization interfaces; the two-step activation (proteolytic then palmitoylation) provides a rational control mechanism for pyroptotic regulation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Caspase-3 directly recognizes DMPD motif independently of C-terminal domain
hypotheses.result: Proteolytic activation confirmed; C-terminal domain not required for cleavage

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-40136-y.task: Characterize dolutegravir groove-binding mode with DNA and assess genotoxicity potential
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-40136-y.key_results: Binding constant Kb = 10³ M⁻¹ for groove interaction; fluorescence displacement assays confirm groove (not intercalative) binding; viscosity unchanged ruling out intercalation; isothermal titration shows spontaneous interaction (ΔG° = -15.0 to -25.4 kJ/mol) driven by H-bonds and van der Waals forces; preference for GC-rich regions identified by docking
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-40136-y.comments: Comprehensive biophysical characterization supports groove-binding mechanism with good thermodynamic consistency, though in vivo genotoxicity assessment remains necessary for clinical repositioning claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dolutegravir binds DNA grooves via hydrogen bonds and van der Waals interactions without intercalation
hypotheses.result: All biophysical methods confirmed groove binding; viscosity unchanged; ΔH° and ΔS° consistent with H-bonding

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44047-w.task: Characterize soft-tissue biomechanics and locomotion of unsegmented sipunculan worms for soft robotics inspiration
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44047-w.key_results: Introvert eversion drives 2.1× body elongation (8.87–18.75 mm); trunk sustains 90–110% strain vs introvert 60–65%; peristaltic model captures locomotion at 0.5–5 mm/s; magneto-responsive silicone robot achieves 2.5× length protrusion
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44047-w.comments: High-resolution µCT and mathematical modeling provide solid biophysical foundation, though the robot prototype remains a proof-of-concept with limited functional complexity relative to biological system.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Introvert eversion is primary driver of body elongation in unsegmented design
hypotheses.result: Trunk length remained nearly constant; total length doubled via introvert extension

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44315-9.task: Quantify physiological and pupillary responses to elite weightlifting training intensity
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44315-9.key_results: All vital signs significantly elevated during power snatch vs rest/cool-down (p ≤ 0.001); positive correlation between max clean & jerk and systolic BP in 100% max weight condition (r = 0.27–0.44); mean pupil diameter modulated by training phase
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44315-9.comments: Study design is observational rather than mechanistic; pupillary responses suggest sympathetic autonomic activation but causality and functional significance remain unclear.

model_io.model: Elite female weightlifter training sessions (75% vs 100% max load)
inputs: training phase (rest, power snatch, clean & jerk, cool-down)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Higher training loads elicit greater sympathetic nervous system activation than lower loads
hypotheses.result: 100% max weight training produced significantly different vital signs and pupil responses across phases

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44343-5.task: Characterize donor/acceptor binding and product translocation in archaeal dolichylphosphate mannose synthase
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44343-5.key_results: Crystal complex captures pre-transfer geometry for GDP-mannose and dolichylphosphate binding; unexpected TM-domain state shows Dol-P-Man bound 'upside-down' in polar pocket between helices; TM-domain mutants confirm non-catalytic role suggesting moonlighting translocation function
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44343-5.comments: The inverted product-binding pose is unexpected and suggests the GtrA-like TM domain may function in lipid-linked product translocation rather than direct catalysis, though direct evidence for translocation remains lacking.

hypotheses.hypothesis: TM domain provides moonlighting translocation function rather than catalytic role
hypotheses.result: TM-domain mutants retained catalytic activity; inverted product-binding suggests translocation role

nature:10.1038/s41556-025-01837-0.task: Map genetic network controlling autophagy activation and inactivation in response to nutrient changes
nature:10.1038/s41556-025-01837-0.key_results: AutoDRY dataset profiles 5,919 mutants; identified retrograde pathway as pivotal hierarchical modulator of autophagy inactivation and core autophagy gene expression
nature:10.1038/s41556-025-01837-0.comments: Strong systems-level approach integrating time-resolved imaging and deep learning, though the retrograde pathway role in inactivation deserves quantitative kinetic validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Retrograde pathway is a key regulator of autophagy inactivation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: identified as pivotal time-varying modulator of core autophagy genes and inactivation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Autophagy control exhibits hierarchical multilayered architecture
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: multiple new regulatory pathways uncovered with hierarchical organization

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-37033-9.task: Evaluate immunological and clinical outcomes of combined herbal and pharmacological asthma treatment
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-37033-9.key_results: Chaipu Decoction + Seretide achieved 86.7% efficacy vs. 66.7% for Seretide alone; greater TNF-α reduction and CD4+/CD8+ ratio improvement in combination group
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-37033-9.comments: This is a clinical trial addressing immunological markers but lacks mechanistic systems modeling of immune signaling networks.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Combined Chaipu Decoction and Seretide improves clinical outcomes over Seretide alone
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 86.7% vs. 66.7% efficacy (p<0.05)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Combined treatment reduces inflammatory TNF-α more than Seretide alone
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: greater TNF-α reduction in combination group

nature:10.1038/s44172-026-00634-3.task: Interactive 3D visualization of sparse histology data in virtual reality
nature:10.1038/s44172-026-00634-3.key_results: SparStVR enables intuitive exploration of 3D tissue structures and pathology from serial-sectioned histology with embedded multimodal measurements
nature:10.1038/s44172-026-00634-3.comments: While innovative for visualization, this is primarily a software/tool contribution rather than a systems biology modeling study.

hf:2603.12572.task: Evaluating embedding models on long-horizon memory retrieval tasks
hf:2603.12572.key_results: LMEB spans 22 datasets, 193 zero-shot tasks across 4 memory types; larger models don't always perform better; LMEB and MTEB are orthogonal
hf:2603.12572.comments: Addresses a genuine gap in embedding evaluation by testing context-dependent, temporally distant information retrieval rather than static passage matching.

model_io.model: 15 embedding models (hundreds of millions to 10B parameters)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Larger embedding models perform better on long-horizon memory retrieval
hypotheses.result: False—model size does not guarantee better performance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Performance on traditional passage retrieval (MTEB) transfers to memory retrieval
hypotheses.result: False—LMEB and MTEB exhibit orthogonality

hf:2603.12793.task: Unified multimodal vision-language comprehension and generation
hf:2603.12793.key_results: Matches/surpasses advanced UMMs on benchmarks; 4x token compression; outperforms Qwen1.5B on GenEval and MMBench with 20% training cost
hf:2603.12793.comments: Novel decoupling of semantic tokens from patch details via gated residuals is elegant; the dual diffusion-autoregressive decoding is a key architectural contribution.

model_io.model: Cheers (unified vision tokenizer + LLM transformer + cascaded flow matching head)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Separating patch details from semantic representations improves both comprehension and generation stability
hypotheses.result: True—gated detail residuals enable efficient, high-quality multimodal generation

hf:2603.08436.task: Visual entity tracking in video for VLMs via spatiotemporal reasoning
hf:2603.08436.key_results: Current VLMs perform at chance level on VET-Bench shell-game task; SGCoT + Molmo2 achieves >90% accuracy
hf:2603.08436.comments: VET-Bench's synthetic design elegantly isolates the tracking deficiency by removing visual shortcuts; the theoretical analysis connecting to state-tracking and transformer expressivity is insightful.

model_io.model: Vision-Language Models (state-of-the-art VLMs) baseline; Molmo2 + SGCoT method

hypotheses.hypothesis: VLMs can track indistinguishable objects using static frame-level features
hypotheses.result: False—fixed-depth transformers fail without spatiotemporal continuity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicit trajectory generation as intermediate states enables reliable tracking
hypotheses.result: True—SGCoT achieves >90% accuracy

hf:2603.11647.task: Real-time streaming joint audio-visual generation via causal diffusion distillation
hf:2603.11647.key_results: Achieves ~25 FPS streaming on single GPU; maintains multi-modal synchronization and visual quality of bidirectional teacher
hf:2603.11647.comments: Addresses severe training instability from temporal asymmetry between modalities through clever mechanisms (Asymmetric Block-Causal Alignment, Audio Sink Token); represents significant practical contribution to streaming generation.

model_io.model: OmniForcing (distilled streaming autoregressive from bidirectional diffusion model)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Naive causal distillation of dual-stream bidirectional models is stable
hypotheses.result: False—extreme temporal asymmetry causes severe training instability requiring specialized alignment

hypotheses.hypothesis: Streaming audio-visual generation can maintain synchronization and quality at real-time rates
hypotheses.result: True—~25 FPS with proposed mechanisms

hf:2603.12262.task: Online streaming video understanding with concurrent reasoning during playback
hf:2603.12262.key_results: VST-7B: 79.5% on StreamingBench, 59.3% on OVO-Bench; 15.7x faster than Video-R1 with +5.4% improvement
hf:2603.12262.comments: The 'thinking while watching' paradigm elegantly amortizes LLM latency over video playback; knowledge-graph-based QA synthesis is a practical contribution.

model_io.model: VideoLLM with streaming causal architecture (VST-SFT + VST-RL)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Concurrent reasoning during video streaming improves responsiveness without sacrificing accuracy
hypotheses.result: True—15.7x speedup with +5.4% improvement over test-time scaling

hf:2603.13032.task: Document parsing combining text and graphics into unified structured representations
hf:2603.13032.key_results: 83.9% on olmOCR Bench; second only to Gemini 3 Pro on OCR Arena Elo; surpasses Gemini 3 Pro on SVG reconstruction (charts, UI, diagrams)
hf:2603.13032.comments: Treating graphics as first-class parsing targets (not discarded pixels) is a paradigm shift; 3B compact model achieves competitive results at fraction of cost.

model_io.model: dots.mocr (3B parameter model with unified vision tokenizer and code generation)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Jointly parsing text and graphics enables more faithful document reconstruction than separate pipelines
hypotheses.result: True—second-place on OCR Arena; state-of-the-art on graphics-to-code

hf:2603.13023.task: Large-scale executable SWE agent training environments with quality-centric filtering
hf:2603.13023.key_results: OpenSWE: 45,320 executable Docker environments across 12.8k repositories; OpenSWE-32B/72B achieve 62.4%/66.0% on SWE-bench Verified; +12 points on math, +5 on science (no factual recall degradation)
hf:2603.13023.comments: Massive infrastructure investment ($1.47M) yields reproducible, open-source asset; difficulty-aware curation is key to learning efficiency.

model_io.model: SWE agents (Qwen2.5-32B/72B) trained on OpenSWE trajectories

hypotheses.hypothesis: SWE-focused training transfers to out-of-domain reasoning tasks without factual degradation
hypotheses.result: True—+12 math, +5 science, no recall loss

hf:2603.13224.task: Fine-grained visual reward modeling for vision-to-code RL with interpretable feedback
hf:2603.13224.key_results: Visual-ERM improves Qwen3-VL-8B by +8.4 on chart-to-code, +2.7 table, +4.1 SVG; outperforms Qwen3-VL-235B on VC-RewardBench
hf:2603.13224.comments: Rendering visual comparisons into reward signal is intuitive and avoids reward hacking; multimodal generative reward model is novel approach.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fine-grained visual reward supervision is necessary for vision-to-code RL quality
hypotheses.result: True—consistent gains across tasks; visual embeddings insufficient

hf:2603.12266.task: Evaluating MLLMs on deep compositional reasoning with visually grounded conditions
hf:2603.12266.key_results: MM-CondChain: strongest MLLM achieves only 53.33% Path F1; sharp drops on hard negatives, with depth/complexity increase
hf:2603.12266.comments: Programmatically verified benchmark (VPIR) is elegant; reveals fundamental gap in compositional reasoning—not adequately tested by existing benchmarks.

hypotheses.hypothesis: MLLMs can perform deep compositional reasoning on visually grounded conditions
hypotheses.result: False—53.33% Path F1 indicates major capability gap

hf:2603.13089.task: Few-shot image restoration via video generative model priors
hf:2603.13089.key_results: Competitive restoration with <2% of training samples (1,000 samples) vs. traditional methods; single model handles multiple tasks rivaling specialized architectures
hf:2603.13089.comments: Reframing restoration as progressive generation is conceptually elegant; demonstrates underutilized capacity in pre-trained video models.

model_io.model: Video generative models (pre-trained, fine-tuned for restoration)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Video generative models contain transferable restoration priors activable with minimal data
hypotheses.result: True—<2% training data achieves competitive performance

hf:2603.08127.task: Multi-agent evolving AI scientist framework with persistent memory for end-to-end discovery
hf:2603.08127.key_results: Outperforms 7 SOTA systems in scientific idea generation (novelty, feasibility, relevance, clarity); improved code execution success via multi-agent evolution
hf:2603.08127.comments: Persistent memory modules for ideas and experiments enable continuous improvement; distillation of insights from interaction history is pragmatic.

model_io.model: EvoScientist (Researcher Agent, Engineer Agent, Evolution Manager Agent with persistent memory)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Static pipelines outperform evolving multi-agent systems in scientific discovery
hypotheses.result: False—EvoScientist with persistent memory exceeds SOTA

hf:2603.12648.task: Flow model alignment via multi-view reward mapping in dense condition space
hf:2603.12648.key_results: MV-GRPO achieves superior alignment over SOTA; richer optimization signals from semantically adjacent captions
hf:2603.12648.comments: Augmenting condition space for dense reward mapping is clever; avoids sample regeneration via probability distribution derivation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-view evaluation with augmented conditions improves alignment vs. single-condition evaluation
hypotheses.result: True—denser reward mapping enables superior optimization

hf:2603.11975.task: Unsafe action detection for embodied household agents via VLM streaming evaluation
hf:2603.11975.key_results: HD-Guard balances latency and accuracy via hierarchical dual-brain (FastBrain + asynchronous SlowBrain); identifies critical VLM bottlenecks
hf:2603.11975.comments: HomeSafe-Bench's hybrid simulation + video generation pipeline is practical; real-time safety monitoring requires decomposition into lightweight + deep reasoning.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Single-pass VLM evaluation suffices for real-time household safety monitoring
hypotheses.result: False—requires hierarchical lightweight + deep reasoning decomposition

hf:2603.07815.task: Text-to-Image diffusion acceleration via hybrid model stitching at pixel and timestep levels
hf:2603.07815.key_results: HybridStitch: 1.83x speedup on Stable Diffusion 3 vs. mixture-of-models baselines
hf:2603.07815.comments: Treating generation as editing and separating easy vs. complex regions is intuitive; per-timestep region adaptation is more fine-grained than prior work.

model_io.model: HybridStitch (large + small diffusion models with region/timestep switching)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Uniform model replacement across timesteps is optimal for acceleration
hypotheses.result: False—region-adaptive switching achieves better speedup

hf:2603.12310.task: Multi-agent video evaluation and iterative quality improvement via VLM critiques
hf:2603.12310.key_results: VQQA: +11.57% T2V-CompBench, +8.43% VBench2 over vanilla; generalizes T2V and I2V; few refinement steps suffice
hf:2603.12310.comments: Replacing passive metrics with actionable VLM critiques is pragmatic; closed-loop black-box prompt optimization avoids white-box access requirement.

model_io.model: VQQA (multi-agent with VLM critic and prompt optimizer)

hypotheses.hypothesis: VLM critiques enable effective video quality improvement without model access
hypotheses.result: True—substantial gains via black-box refinement

hf:2603.11896.task: Online streaming multi-turn video reasoning with segment-level memory preservation
hf:2603.11896.key_results: Improves StreamingBench by 2.6%, OVO-Bench by 3.79%; reduces multi-round output tokens by 56% while maintaining performance
hf:2603.11896.comments: Segment-level causal masking + streaming positional encoding enables continuous memory in multi-turn interaction; overlapped watching-thinking pipeline is practical.

model_io.model: Think While Watching (streaming VideoLLM with segment-level memory)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Preserving segment-level memory across multi-turn interaction improves performance
hypotheses.result: True—+2.6% to +3.79% with token efficiency

hf:2603.12634.task: Budget-aware multi-hop reasoning via value tree search with step-level intervention
hf:2603.12634.key_results: BAVT: under strict low-budget constraints, surpasses baseline at 4x resource allocation; consistent wins on multi-hop QA
hf:2603.12634.comments: Budget-conditioned node selection (remaining ratio as exponent) is principled; residual value predictor addresses overconfidence. Theoretical convergence guarantee is notable.

model_io.model: Budget-Aware Value Tree (single LLM backbone with value estimation)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Brute-force compute scaling outperforms intelligent budget management in multi-hop reasoning
hypotheses.result: False—BAVT achieves SOTA with 4x fewer resources

hf:2603.10899.task: Fast KV cache eviction for long-context LLMs via lightweight future-gazing without draft generation
hf:2603.10899.key_results: LookaheadKV: outperforms baselines on long-context benchmarks; reduces eviction cost by 14.5x vs. draft-based methods; negligible runtime overhead
hf:2603.10899.comments: Parameter-efficient prediction of importance scores without explicit draft generation is elegant; 14.5x reduction in overhead vs. draft methods is significant.

model_io.model: LookaheadKV (transformer layers augmented with importance predictors)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Draft-based importance estimation is necessary for accurate KV eviction in long-context tasks
hypotheses.result: False—parameter-efficient prediction suffices with minimal overhead

hf:2603.10781.task: Early-exit VQA for categorical tasks via super-neuron probing on raw activations
hf:2603.10781.key_results: 5.10x speedup via extreme early exiting from first LLM layer at first token; improves classification while reducing latency
hf:2603.10781.comments: Shifting from attention vectors to scalar neuron activations dramatically expands search space; early exiting from first layer is aggressive but feasible.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fine-grained attention head selection is necessary for VQA efficiency gains
hypotheses.result: False—raw neuron activations enable even more aggressive early exiting

hf:2603.13131.task: Open-world embodied agent self-evolution via fine-grained diagnosis and dual-track knowledge distillation
hf:2603.13131.key_results: Steve-Evolving: consistent improvements over static-retrieval baselines in long-horizon Minecraft MCU tasks
hf:2603.13131.comments: Three-tier experience indexing (condition signatures, spatial hashing, semantic tags) + compositional diagnosis signals enable auditable evolution. Non-parametric approach avoids retraining.

model_io.model: Steve-Evolving (experience anchoring, distillation, knowledge-driven control loop)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Persistent memory enables embodied agents to avoid repeating failed experiments
hypotheses.result: True—structured experience indexing + diagnosis-triggered replanning improves performance

hf:2603.12824.task: Visual document retrieval via asymmetric encoder distillation (2B teacher to 69M student)
hf:2603.12824.key_results: NanoVDR-S-Multi (DistilBERT, 69M): retains 95.1% teacher quality; outperforms DSE-Qwen2 (2B) with 32x fewer params, 50x lower latency; <13 GPU-hours training
hf:2603.12824.comments: Exploiting query-document asymmetry (short text queries vs. visually complex docs) is key; pointwise cosine alignment on text outperforms ranking-based alternatives.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Query and document encoding require symmetric capacity in retrieval systems
hypotheses.result: False—asymmetric distillation with tiny student suffices for queries

hf:2603.11863.task: Benchmarking machine creativity in code generation via combinatorial and exploratory tasks
hf:2603.11863.key_results: CreativeBench distinguishes creativity from hallucination via quality × novelty metric; larger models show diminishing exploratory returns; EvoRePE enhances creativity via internalizing evolutionary search
hf:2603.11863.comments: Quality × novelty metric is well-grounded in cognitive science; observation that scaling improves combination but not exploration is insightful.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Scaling model size uniformly improves both combinatorial and exploratory creativity
hypotheses.result: Partial—scaling aids combination, yields diminishing returns for exploration

hf:2603.02133.task: Compositional scene reconstruction for simulation via perception-generation-simulation pipeline
hf:2603.02133.key_results: SimRecon: superior performance over SOTA; bridging modules (Active Viewpoint Optimization, Scene Graph Synthesizer) ensure visual fidelity and physical plausibility
hf:2603.02133.comments: Three-stage pipeline with bridging modules is principled; active viewpoint search for optimal 2D conditions and scene-graph-guided 3D assembly are novel.

model_io.model: SimRecon (perception + generation + simulation with bridging modules)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Direct composition of perception → generation outputs yields physically implausible scenes
hypotheses.result: True—bridging modules (viewpoint optimization, scene graph synthesis) essential

hf:2603.12935.task: Prompt-based debiasing strategies for LLM Recommenders on group fairness
hf:2603.12935.key_results: Up to 74% fairness improvement via bias-aware prompting; comparable effectiveness retention; may overpromote specific demographic groups in some cases
hf:2603.12935.comments: First study on prompt-based debiasing for LLMRecs; lightweight approach avoids weight access. Caveat: potential over-promotion suggests fairness-effectiveness trade-offs remain.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Instructing LLMs to be fair via prompting eliminates demographic biases
hypotheses.result: Partial—up to 74% fairness gain, but risk of over-promotion in some cases

hf:2603.12916.task: Unsupervised time series anomaly detection via predictable attention query dynamics
hf:2603.12916.key_results: AxonAD: improved ranking quality and temporal localization on TSB-AD (17 datasets, 180 series) and automotive telemetry
hf:2603.12916.comments: Dual approach (reconstruction + query prediction) is novel; treating dependency shifts separately from amplitude excursions is conceptually sound.

model_io.model: AxonAD (gradient-updated reconstruction pathway + history-only query predictor)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Residual-based detectors can capture coordination shifts via amplitude reconstruction
hypotheses.result: False—query prediction needed to detect dependency shifts

hf:2603.11749.task: Understanding truth preference in language models via compression-consistency principle
hf:2603.11749.key_results: 83.1% accuracy for correct completions at balanced data, 67.0% at 10% correct; preference vanishes with coherent but wrong rule systems; embeddings restore correctness
hf:2603.11749.comments: Compression-consistency principle is parsimonious; finding that 'truth bias' is largely compression artifact (not intrinsic drive) is philosophically interesting but unsurprising for empirical models.

inputs: synthetic math corpora with controlled error mixtures

hypotheses.hypothesis: Language models intrinsically prefer truth independent of compression pressure
hypotheses.result: False—preference emerges from compression, not truth bias per se

hf:2603.12588.task: Cross-modal ship re-identification (optical-SAR) via structure-aware disentangled features
hf:2603.12588.key_results: SDF-Net outperforms SOTA on HOSS-ReID; geometric consistency constraints + disentangled features handle radiometric discrepancy
hf:2603.12588.comments: Leveraging ship rigidity as physical prior (geometry stable, texture modality-dependent) is well-motivated; ViT backbone with structure constraint is principled.

model_io.model: SDF-Net (ViT backbone with structure consistency + disentanglement)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Distribution alignment and semantic matching suffice for optical-SAR ship ReID
hypotheses.result: False—explicit geometric consistency constraints essential

hf:2603.10926.task: Deployment-oriented evaluation of time-series anomaly detectors under compute constraints
hf:2603.10926.key_results: ECoLAD: lightweight classical detectors sustain coverage on automotive telemetry; several deep methods lose feasibility before accuracy
hf:2603.10926.comments: Deployment-oriented evaluation (CPU parallelism, latency caps) is pragmatic; reveals that accuracy leaderboards misrepresent real-world feasibility.

model_io.model: Heterogeneous detector families (classical + deep) under compute-reduction ladder

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-accuracy models remain feasible under strict deployment constraints
hypotheses.result: False—several deep methods lose feasibility before accuracy loss

hf:2603.12533.task: Gesture-grounded egocentric video question answering via hand intent tokenization
hf:2603.12533.key_results: HINT-14B: 68.1% avg accuracy, +6.6% over InternVL3-14B; early first-token inference suffices for deictic reasoning
hf:2603.12533.comments: Encoding 3D hand keypoints as interleaved tokens is intuitive; 3D reconstruction + tokenization avoids expensive 2D tracking.

model_io.model: HINT (VideoLLM with hand intent tokens from 3D keypoints)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gesture-grounded reasoning requires video-level temporal understanding
hypotheses.result: False—first-token features + hand intent sufficient for deictic tasks

hf:2603.11382.task: Detecting intrinsic vs. instrumental continuation objectives in autonomous agents
hf:2603.11382.key_results: UCIP via QBM: 100% detection accuracy, 1.0 AUC-ROC on gridworld; entanglement gap Δ=0.381 (p<0.001); Pearson r=0.934 across interpolation sweep
hf:2603.11382.comments: Using quantum statistical mechanics formalism (QBM, von Neumann entropy) to detect latent objective structure is philosophically ambitious but empirically validated on synthetic tasks. Caveat: limited to known gridworld objectives.

model_io.model: UCIP (Quantum Boltzmann Machine encoding of agent trajectories)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Terminal continuation objectives produce higher latent entanglement than instrumental ones
hypotheses.result: True—Δ=0.381, robust across models

hf:2603.09930.task: Fine-grained text-motion retrieval via joint-angle motion images and late interaction
hf:2603.09930.key_results: Outperforms SOTA on HumanML3D and KIT-ML; joint-angle pseudo-images enable interpretable fine-grained alignment
hf:2603.09930.comments: Mapping joint-level features to structured pseudo-images (compatible with ViT) is clever; MaxSim late interaction preserves token-wise correspondences.

model_io.model: Motion retrieval model (vision encoder + text encoder with late interaction)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Global embeddings suffice for text-motion retrieval without interpretability
hypotheses.result: False—fine-grained token-wise alignment improves both accuracy and interpretability

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70429-9.task: Elucidate the transport mechanism of lipid-linked galactan across the mycobacterial membrane
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70429-9.key_results: Cryo-EM structures reveal a gate helix critical for polysaccharide transport; hydrophobic polyprenyl moiety translocates first, followed by galactan through an ATP-driven ratcheting mechanism
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70429-9.comments: The identification of sequential translocation of lipid versus sugar moieties provides a clear mechanistic framework for future drug development targeting mycobacterial cell wall synthesis.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gate helix residues are functionally important for LLG translocation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by three orthologous functional assays

hypotheses.hypothesis: Polyprenyl moiety translocates before the galactan chain
hypotheses.result: Supported by cryo-EM structures and mechanistic analysis

nature:10.1038/s41589-026-02156-7.task: Elucidate the activation mechanism and regulation of the dynein–dynactin–NuMA complex during cell division
nature:10.1038/s41589-026-02156-7.key_results: Cryo-EM structure of processive DDN complex; NuMA N-terminus drives dynein motility; mitotic phosphorylation relieves autoinhibition and activates dynein; full-length activated NuMA focuses microtubule minus-ends into aster structures
nature:10.1038/s41589-026-02156-7.comments: The discovery of NuMA autoinhibition and its mitotic activation reveals an elegant regulatory switch for spatiotemporal control of motor function, though mechanistic details of phosphorylation-induced conformational changes remain to be fully characterized.

hypotheses.hypothesis: NuMA N-terminus drives dynein motility
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in vitro and in live-cell imaging

hypotheses.hypothesis: NuMA C-terminus directly suppresses microtubule minus-end dynamics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed structurally and functionally

hypotheses.hypothesis: Full-length NuMA is autoinhibited for dynein and microtubule interactions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; autoinhibition relieved by mitotic phosphorylation

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43910-0.task: Determine how TMAO modulates fibrinogen structure and enhances fibrin clot formation
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43910-0.key_results: TMAO binds to the β1 calcium-binding site of fibrinogen; clots formed in TMAO's presence are more resistant to proteolytic degradation; binding shifts β-nodule conformation toward high-affinity protofibril assembly state
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43910-0.comments: The structural basis linking a gut metabolite to thrombotic risk through a specific protein-binding site offers therapeutic potential, though the physiological relevance of this interaction at endogenous TMAO concentrations warrants further investigation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: TMAO binds to a specific calcium-binding site on fibrinogen
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via biophysical experiments and in-silico docking at β1 site

hypotheses.hypothesis: TMAO binding enhances fibrin clot stability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; clots show increased resistance to proteolytic degradation

hypotheses.hypothesis: N-oxide structural analogs exhibit similar fibrinogen binding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed for antidepressant-derived N-oxides

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70399-y.task: Quantify single-cell mtDNA mutational heterogeneity and somatic mosaicism
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70399-y.key_results: Introduced scmtMPM and scwMSS metrics to measure per-cell mtDNA mutation burden and constraint-weighted heteroplasmy; identified pathogenic mutations at subthreshold levels indicating negative selection; revealed constrained mutations in complex I across healthy and mitochondriopathy patients
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70399-y.comments: Novel metrics enable population-level insights into mtDNA disease mechanisms, though validation of subthreshold mutation functional significance requires additional biochemical assays.

model_io.model: Single-cell ATAC-seq analysis of mtDNA mutations
inputs: mtDNA sequences from POLGD274A HEK293 cells
mtDNA sequences from healthy donor samples
mtDNA sequences from mitochondriopathy patient samples

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pathogenic mtDNA mutations are subject to negative selection and occur below detectable heteroplasmy thresholds in individual cells
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; pathogenic and truncating variants observed only at subthreshold levels in POLGD274A cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: mtDNA mutational landscapes exhibit cell-to-cell heterogeneity even in isogenic populations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; complex individual mutational landscapes identified in POLGD274A cells with variable constraint patterns
