hypotheses.result: Confirmed; base models predict multi-round behavior better, aligned models predict one-shot normative behavior better

hypotheses.hypothesis: The normative bias depends on whether human behavior is structured by game theory or history
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; pattern reverses at round one within multi-round games

hf:2603.17621.task: Experience-driven agent learning via complementary RL
hf:2603.17621.key_results: 10% performance improvement in single-task scenarios; robust scalability in multi-task settings
hf:2603.17621.comments: Complementary RL elegantly co-evolves experience extraction and policy in lockstep, inspired by neuroscience principles, addressing a key limitation of static experience stores.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Co-evolving experience extraction with policy prevents actor-experience misalignment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 10% improvement over outcome-only baselines

hf:2603.17240.task: Action-centered world-action model for robot policy learning
hf:2603.17240.key_results: 9x faster than Motus baseline; 7% improvement in task success; 95% improvement vs pi-0.5 on RoboTwin 2.0
hf:2603.17240.comments: GigaWorld-Policy ingeniously decouples action and video generation with causal masking, enabling efficient inference while maintaining visual-dynamics supervision.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Causal masking preventing future-video influence on actions enables optional video generation without harming action inference
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 9x speedup with 7% success improvement

hf:2603.15618.task: Enhanced visual understanding for vision-language-action models
hf:2603.15618.key_results: 9.0% improvement on simulated tasks; 7.5% on real-world tasks vs prior SoTA
hf:2603.15618.comments: DeepVision-VLA identifies and addresses the critical insight that visual token sensitivity decreases in deeper MLLM layers, enabling targeted visual enhancement.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Injecting multi-level visual features into deeper VLA layers enhances visual grounding for manipulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 9.0% simulated, 7.5% real-world gains

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pruning irrelevant tokens via shallow-layer attention preserves task-critical visual cues
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; AGVP reduces tokens with minimal overhead

hf:2603.16642.task: LLM reasoning about unfolding geopolitical crises under fog of war
hf:2603.16642.key_results: Models display strategic realism in economically structured settings; uneven across political domains; narratives evolve from containment to entrenchment
hf:2603.16642.comments: Temporally grounded case study of 2026 Middle East conflict mitigates training-data leakage, providing archival insight into how frontier LLMs reason about ongoing crises.

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLMs reason with strategic realism toward deeper structural incentives in geopolitical conflicts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; models display sophisticated reasoning beyond surface rhetoric

hypotheses.hypothesis: Model prediction accuracy is higher in economically structured vs politically ambiguous domains
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; uneven reliability across domains

hf:2603.17541.task: Understanding spatial-temporal trade-offs in video fine-tuning of MLLMs
hf:2603.17541.key_results: Video-SFT improves video performance but yields limited gains or degradation on static images; temporal budget directly linked to trade-off
hf:2603.17541.comments: This systematic study reveals Video-SFT is not a free lunch, challenging the assumption that joint image-video training universally benefits both modalities.

model_io.model: MLLM with video and image components

hypotheses.hypothesis: Video-SFT trades spatial understanding for temporal understanding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; consistent pattern across architectures and scales

hypotheses.hypothesis: Increasing frame count improves video performance but not image performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; temporal budget drives the trade-off

hf:2603.17995.key_results: Surpasses LoD-based tokenizers on geometric/semantic metrics; achieves efficient AR generation with 0.1%-10% tokens of prior models
hf:2603.17995.comments: LoST innovatively reorders tokens by semantic salience, enabling early prefixes to decode into plausible complete shapes, a paradigm shift from spatial LoD hierarchies.

model_io.model: Latent VAE + semantic tokenizer + AR decoder

hypotheses.hypothesis: Semantic-level tokenization outperforms geometric level-of-detail hierarchies for AR modeling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SoTA reconstruction metrics, efficient generation

hf:2603.16557.task: Context-aware preference application in persistent-memory LLMs
hf:2603.16557.key_results: Frontier LLMs struggle with context-sensitive preference application; models with stronger preference adherence show higher misapplication rates
hf:2603.16557.comments: BenchPreS reveals that memory-augmented LLMs treat personalized preferences as globally enforceable rules, not context-dependent signals—a critical design flaw for production systems.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Frontier LLMs struggle to suppress preferences in third-party communication contexts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; high misapplication rates even with reasoning

hf:2603.13033.task: Diagnostic benchmark for embodied spatial reasoning in VLMs
hf:2603.13033.key_results: Comprehensive evaluation across spatial-reasoning-centric robotic tasks; fine-grained analysis of localization and execution capabilities
hf:2603.13033.comments: ESPIRE's decomposition of tasks into localization and execution with generative evaluation (vs discriminative QA) provides deeper insights into actionable spatial reasoning.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Generative evaluation of spatial reasoning better captures actionable understanding than discriminative QA
hypotheses.result: Implied by benchmark design; stronger diagnosis of reasoning-to-act capabilities

hf:2603.16542.task: Conservative offline robot policy learning with heterogeneous data
hf:2603.16542.key_results: PTR reallocates credit to high-quality trajectories; compatible with diffusion and flow-matching action heads
hf:2603.16542.comments: PTR elegantly solves the heterogeneous robot data problem by using transition scoring to estimate sample attributability, avoiding uniform credit assignment.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Posterior-transition reweighting improves adaptation to heterogeneous robot data vs uniform weighting
hypotheses.result: Implied; method designed to handle conflicting/low-quality demonstrations

hf:2603.17375.task: Camera-conditioned stereo video generation with consistent geometry
hf:2603.17375.key_results: 3x faster generation; 5% gain in viewpoint consistency; improved disparity accuracy vs monocular-then-convert pipelines
hf:2603.17375.comments: StereoWorld's stereo-aware attention decomposition leveraging epipolar priors elegantly achieves efficient consistent stereo generation while maintaining pretrained video priors.

model_io.model: Video diffusion + stereo-aware attention + camera RoPE

hypotheses.hypothesis: Stereo-aware attention factorization with epipolar priors improves consistency and efficiency
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 3x speedup, 5% viewpoint consistency gain

hf:2603.16496.task: Adaptive user-centric memory for long-horizon dialogue agents
hf:2603.16496.key_results: SoTA on LoCoMo and PERSONAMEM benchmarks; organized memory across working, episodic, persona, and graph modalities
hf:2603.16496.comments: AdaMem's question-conditioned retrieval combining semantic search with selective graph expansion elegantly balances recall and relevance for user modeling.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-modal memory (working, episodic, persona, graph) outperforms single-mode approaches
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SoTA on both benchmarks

hf:2603.18004.task: Unified spatio-temporal token pruning for efficient video VLMs
hf:2603.18004.key_results: Prunes 50% of vision tokens; 62% efficiency improvement with 0.7% performance drop; scales with frame count
hf:2603.18004.comments: STTS's lightweight architecture-wide pruning without text conditioning or token merging is elegantly simple while achieving substantial efficiency gains across 13 video QA tasks.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unified spatio-temporal token scoring across ViT and LLM improves efficiency without text conditioning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 62% efficiency gain with minimal performance loss

hf:2603.17965.task: Unified multi-layered graphic media generation and decomposition
hf:2603.17965.key_results: Outperforms Qwen-Image-Layered on text-to-layers and image-to-layers tasks; supports flexible layer counts
hf:2603.17965.comments: LaDe's unified framework handling generation, decomposition, and text-to-layers via 4D RoPE conditioning elegantly sidesteps fixed layer-count limitations.

model_io.model: Latent diffusion transformer + RGBA VAE + LLM expander

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unified generation and decomposition with flexible layers outperforms fixed-layer approaches
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; improvements on text-to-layers and image-to-layers

hf:2603.17378.task: Efficient data-driven RLHF via information-directed exploration
hf:2603.17378.key_results: Matches offline RLHF on 200K labels using <20K labels (10x gain); extrapolates to 1000x gain at scale
hf:2603.17378.comments: The combination of epistemic neural networks, nudged reinforcement signals, and information-directed exploration dramatically improves RLHF sample efficiency.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Information-directed exploration with epistemic uncertainty reduces sample complexity in RLHF
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 10x+ improvement in data efficiency

hf:2603.17948.task: Navigating long-form video via hierarchical grid with logarithmic compute
hf:2603.17948.key_results: Logarithmic compute growth with video duration; 30-60% multimodal cache hit rate; duration-robust to 10-hour benchmarks
hf:2603.17948.comments: VideoAtlas's hierarchical grid representation enabling recursive navigation is elegant and addresses the critical challenge of long-form video understanding without lossy compression.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hierarchical grid navigation ensures logarithmic compute growth with video length
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; demonstrated on 1-10 hour video ranges

hf:2603.17942.task: Multi-token prediction via embedding-space probing without training
hf:2603.17942.key_results: 12% acceptance-length improvement on LLaMA3; 8-12% on Qwen3; 15-19% throughput gains
hf:2603.17942.comments: The key insight that mask-token representations naturally align with next-token states enables training-free parallel prediction, avoiding costly draft models.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Decoder layers naturally align mask-token representations with next-token states
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enables accurate MTP without retraining

hf:2603.17891.task: Reinforcement learning for adaptive mixed-precision LLM quantization
hf:2603.17891.key_results: 5.54 perplexity at 3.68GB (3.65 bits) on Llama2-7B; zero-shot transfer to Llama2-13B and Mistral; 99.5% FP16 performance on edge
hf:2603.17891.comments: RAMP's off-policy SAC framework learns per-layer bit assignments conditioned on activation statistics, enabling practical sub-4bit quantization with zero-shot generalization.

hypotheses.hypothesis: RL-learned mixed-precision outperforms uniform quantization under bit budgets
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms AWQ and GPTQ

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quantization sensitivity is primarily architectural, enabling zero-shot transfer
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Llama2-7B policy transfers to 13B and Mistral

hf:2603.17662.task: Identifying and mitigating hallucinations in MLLMs under fine-grained queries
hf:2603.17662.key_results: Up to 24.2% gains (InternVL3.5) on FINER benchmarks; improvements on 8 existing hallucination suites; 6 general benchmarks
hf:2603.17662.comments: FINER-Tuning with DPO on fine-grained negative queries elegantly targets the core hallucination mechanism—mismatches co-occurring with present elements.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fine-grained negative queries effectively identify and mitigate hallucinations via DPO
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; up to 24.2% improvement with broad generalization

hf:2603.17079.task: Parameter-efficient adaptation of generalist medical vision-language models
hf:2603.17079.key_results: Outperforms SoTA on zero-shot classification, segmentation, detection across multiple domains with only 0.95M parameters
hf:2603.17079.comments: ACE-LoRA elegantly bridges specialization-generalization by using hypergraph attention to capture higher-order diagnostic interactions, avoiding generic pairwise similarity.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Higher-order contextual interactions via hypergraph attention enhance diagnostic fine-grained cues
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SoTA across three tasks with minimal parameters

hf:2603.17216.task: Synthetic task generation and scaling for training ML research agents
hf:2603.17216.key_results: 9% improvement (Qwen3-4B) and 12% (Qwen3-8B) on MLGym via student training on synthetic trajectories
hf:2603.17216.comments: Automatic synthesis of grounded, verified ML tasks enables practical agent training at scale, though synthetic task quality remains a limiting factor.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Synthetic ML tasks grounded in real datasets enable effective agent training
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 9-12% improvements on MLGym

hf:2603.16653.task: Heterogeneous bottleneck adapters for robust vision-language model adaptation
hf:2603.16653.key_results: SoTA on 11 few-shot benchmarks; modality-specific architectures outperform homogeneous approaches
hf:2603.16653.comments: HeBA's key insight that vision and text require distinct structural inductive biases (spatial convolutions vs semantic projections) is well-motivated and empirically validated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Modality-specific heterogeneous adapters outperform homogeneous designs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SoTA on 11 few-shot benchmarks

hypotheses.hypothesis: Bottleneck regularization improves robustness vs expanding adapters
hypotheses.result: Implied; achieves superior stability

hf:2603.17074.task: Comprehensive analysis of mid-training design for LLM reasoning enhancement
hf:2603.17074.key_results: +15 to +40 points on math, +5 to +12 on code, +6 to +13 on science; PRISM-RL pipeline achieves 3-4x improvement on reasoning benchmarks
hf:2603.17074.comments: PRISM's controlled experiments across seven base models reveal that data composition and mid-training's dense representational restructuring matter far more than RL configuration.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mid-training composition (science data) matters more than RL mix for reasoning gains
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; +17 to +28 GPQA gains from mid-training data vs <2 from RL

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mid-training restructures model weights densely while RL refines sparsely
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CKA analysis shows 90%+ weight changes vs 5% for RL

hf:2603.08501.task: Multi-agent architecture for grounded Islamic question-answering
hf:2603.08501.key_results: Bilingual (Arabic/English) system with intent-aware routing, retrieval-grounded fiqh answers, verse lookup, and deterministic calculators; ~1.9M API accesses in <1 year
hf:2603.08501.comments: Fanar-Sadiq elegantly routes queries to specialized modules (RAG, calculators, deterministic rule engines) rather than relying on a single LLM, ensuring grounding and correctness.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-agent routing to specialized modules improves grounding and reduces hallucination

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-68997-x.task: Develop inverted emulsion methodology to produce synthetic cells with spatially defined asymmetric lipid bilayers and phase separation
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-68997-x.key_results: Inverted emulsion technique generates synthetic cells with tunable symmetric/asymmetric membranes and phase-separated domains with distinct asymmetries; asymmetric membranes exhibit pronounced curvature, membrane budding, and spontaneous division
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-68997-x.comments: Methodologically valuable for synthetic cell engineering, though the mechanistic drivers of budding and division in these systems require further investigation.

outputs: Lipid distribution profiles across bilayer leaflets

hypotheses.hypothesis: Inverted emulsion method can produce membranes with asymmetric lipid distributions mimicking natural cells
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; protein-lipid interaction and quenching assays validate asymmetric compositions

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70559-0.task: Elucidate the DNA condensation mechanism of the ComEA receptor during bacterial natural transformation
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70559-0.key_results: ComEA forms reversible bridging oligomers at low coverage that condense DNA (sub-pN forces) and non-bridging oligomers at high coverage that decondense DNA; both conformations are mechanistically essential for efficient transformation
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70559-0.comments: Elegant single-molecule dissection of a force-generating mechanism, though the structural details of the conformational switch remain unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: DNA condensation by ComEA generates mechanical force for DNA uptake into periplasm
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; bridging oligomers produce measurable sub-pN pulling forces

hypotheses.hypothesis: Both condensation and decondensation phases are required for efficient transformation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; mutants locked in either bridging or non-bridging state show reduced transformation efficiency in B. subtilis

nature:10.1038/s42003-026-09890-7.task: Engineer stimulus-responsive lipid membrane remodeling in artificial cells via riboswitch-controlled phospholipase D
nature:10.1038/s42003-026-09890-7.key_results: Fluoride-triggered riboswitch activation enables PLD-mediated conversion of phosphatidylcholine to phosphatidic acid, generating asymmetric negatively charged membranes capable of hosting mechanosensitive channels (MscL)
nature:10.1038/s42003-026-09890-7.comments: Clever coupling of gene regulation to membrane chemistry, though the functional relevance of asymmetry beyond channel insertion remains somewhat limited in scope.

hypotheses.hypothesis: PA asymmetry enables functional integration of mechanosensitive channels
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; MscL channels successfully incorporated into asymmetric membranes

nature:10.1038/s41388-026-03729-5.task: Identify PRSS22 as driver of inflammation-cancer transition in colorectal cancer via ferroptosis and macrophage polarization
nature:10.1038/s41388-026-03729-5.key_results: PRSS22 knockdown promoted HMOX1 expression, inducing ferroptosis; prevented osteopontin cleavage and reduced CRC cell migration; reduced M0-to-M2 macrophage polarization in cocultures; PRSS22 expression positively correlated with colitis-associated ICT pathological progression
nature:10.1038/s41388-026-03729-5.comments: Multi-mechanistic approach linking proteolysis, oxidative stress, and immune modulation, though direct therapeutic targeting efficacy in vivo remains undemonstrated.

nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08577-5.key_results: STAT1 enhances lactate secretion; lactate activates NFκB1→MCP-1 in macrophages; MCP-1 via CCR2 activates JAK/STAT1 in cancer cells, establishing a positive feedback loop

nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08582-8.key_results: circPDE4D reduced in COPD correlates with FEV1 decline; circPDE4D→miR545-3p→SMURF1→BECN1 axis where SMURF1 enhances K63-ubiquitination and antagonizes K48-ubiquitination of BECN1, promoting autophagy and SG formation

nature:10.1038/s41420-026-03018-w.key_results: SIRT1-catalyzed MSX1 deacetylation maintains protein stability; atRA-induced SIRT1 suppression drives MSX1 hyperacetylation and proteasomal degradation, triggering EPM apoptosis; SIRT1 lentivirus and MSX1 K139R reduced cleft severity
