hypotheses.result: Confirmed: strongest overall performance on VQA/grounding

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dense-task tuning improves both backbone families
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: detection/segmentation training beneficial

hypotheses.hypothesis: Higher ImageNet accuracy does not guarantee VLM gains
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: backbone scale/accuracy poorly predictive of VLM performance

hf:2603.17735.task: Texture synthesis and reconstruction for untextured 3D models via turntable video generation
hf:2603.17735.key_results: High-fidelity 360-degree turntable videos with strict geometric consistency; 3D reconstruction from TTV; multi-stage inpainting for self-occluded regions
hf:2603.17735.comments: Clever geometry-conditioned video diffusion but abstract lacks quantitative metrics (chamfer distance, texture quality) and comparison to texture synthesis baselines.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Geometry-conditioned diffusion maintains 3D consistency better than unconditioned generation
hypotheses.result: Inferred confirmed from design; quantitative validation not in abstract

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-stage inpainting effectively handles self-occlusion
hypotheses.result: Inferred from pipeline design; metric results absent

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70569-y.task: Investigate how hydrodynamic flow interactions regulate school cohesion and information propagation in large fish groups
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70569-y.key_results: Flow interactions destabilize schools >50,000 fish, creating scale-free correlations in cohesive clusters but decreasing correlation length before fragmentation; directional information propagates linearly in time via non-reciprocal visual interactions
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70569-y.comments: Novel finding that fragmentation precedes information loss provides mechanistic insight into collective vulnerability, though causality between flow and group regulation remains partially inferred.

model_io.model: Computational fish school model (up to 50,000 individuals)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Scale-free correlations persist independent of group size
hypotheses.result: Partially confirmed; scale-free in cohesive clusters but correlation length decreases during fragmentation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Flow interactions regulate group size and behavioral adaptation
hypotheses.result: Supported; flow destabilization creates clustering dynamics similar to natural schools

hypotheses.hypothesis: Information transfer rate depends on cluster topology
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; merging speeds up while fragmentation slows down information propagation

nature:10.1038/s41563-026-02553-2.task: Determine molecular and biophysical mechanisms controlling epithelial-to-fluid transition in breast cancer
nature:10.1038/s41563-026-02553-2.key_results: IRSp53 depletion increases cell fluidity and active wetting via decreased intercellular friction; IRSp53-Afadin interaction controls global tensile state; low IRSp53 expression correlates with worse clinical outcomes in breast cancer patients
nature:10.1038/s41563-026-02553-2.comments: Strong link between single protein mechanotransduction and clinical prognosis; however, the specific role of Afadin versus other junctional proteins in regulating viscosity needs further dissection.

model_io.model: Breast cancer cell monolayers and spheroids

hypotheses.hypothesis: IRSp53 regulates epithelial fluidity through membrane-cytoskeleton linkage
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; IRSp53 depletion increases fluidity in 2D and 3D models

hypotheses.hypothesis: IRSp53-Afadin interaction controls global tensile state and wetting behavior
hypotheses.result: Confirmed through molecular and mechanical measurements

hypotheses.hypothesis: Low IRSp53 expression predicts poor clinical outcome in breast cancer
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in patient samples; aberrant localization also associated with worse prognosis

nature:10.1038/s41592-026-03033-w.task: Predict three-dimensional structures of complex carbohydrates for molecular modeling and simulation
nature:10.1038/s41592-026-03033-w.key_results: GLYCAM-Web provides online tools for generating experimentally consistent 3D structures of oligosaccharides, glycans, polysaccharides, and glycoproteins linked to external databases including PDB
nature:10.1038/s41592-026-03033-w.comments: Useful computational resource for carbohydrate structure prediction, but the abstract lacks quantitative validation metrics or comparison with experimental structures.

nature:10.1038/s43246-026-01125-w.task: Fabricate multifunctional nanomaterials for simultaneous tumor cell detection and photothermal ablation
nature:10.1038/s43246-026-01125-w.key_results: Metallic alloy nanomushrooms on nanowires demonstrate enhanced fluorescence for visualization and photothermal conversion for tumor cell elimination in a single theranostic agent
nature:10.1038/s43246-026-01125-w.comments: The work is materials-focused with biomedical application rather than mechanistic biophysics; in vivo efficacy and comparison to existing photothermal agents would strengthen claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mushroom morphology enables dense targeting molecule loading while alloy core improves photothermal conversion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed through experimental validation as theranostic agent

nature:10.1038/s44303-026-00141-2.task: Develop high-speed, large field-of-view light-sheet microscope for super-resolution imaging of expanded samples
nature:10.1038/s44303-026-00141-2.key_results: pan-ASLM achieves 586 nm lateral and 428 nm axial resolution over 640×640 µm² field of view at 20 fps (1200× faster than confocal, 7× larger field of view, 2× better axial resolution)
nature:10.1038/s44303-026-00141-2.comments: Excellent technical advancement for expansion microscopy; validation on HeLa cells and tissue samples demonstrates practical utility but lacks direct comparison with competing light-sheet designs.

inputs: expanded biological samples (HeLa cells, mouse kidney/brain tissue)

hypotheses.hypothesis: ASLM design enables simultaneous large field of view and high axial resolution for expanded samples
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; pan-ASLM validates design across multiple tissue types

nature:10.1038/s41392-026-02615-6.task: Identify VILIP-1 role in atrial calcium dysregulation and arrhythmia susceptibility
nature:10.1038/s41392-026-02615-6.key_results: VILIP-1 upregulated in AF tissues; cardiomyocyte-specific overexpression promotes Ca²⁺ leakage, DADs, and APD alternans; VILIP-1 enhances NCX-1 surface abundance via myristoylation-dependent trafficking
nature:10.1038/s41392-026-02615-6.comments: Comprehensive mechanistic study with FDA-approved drug repurposing (repaglinide, desloratadine); validation of drug efficacy in human iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes would strengthen translational impact.

model_io.model: Human AF patient atrial tissue; rat pacing-induced AF model; atrial cardiomyocyte-specific VILIP-1 transgenic mice

hypotheses.hypothesis: VILIP-1 disrupts atrial calcium homeostasis by promoting NCX-1 surface trafficking via myristoylation, fostering AF susceptibility
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: VILIP-1 overexpression causes pathological Ca²⁺ leakage and arrhythmia phenotypes; FDA-approved drugs targeting VILIP-1 or myristoylation attenuate AF in human/rodent models

nature:10.1038/s41417-026-01021-y.task: Characterize ferroptosis resistance mechanisms in osteosarcoma
nature:10.1038/s41417-026-01021-y.key_results: GPX4 inhibition (RSL3) triggers compensatory NRF2 activation, iron depletion (ferritin/ferroportin upregulation), and survival pathway activation; combined targeting overcomes resistance
nature:10.1038/s41417-026-01021-y.comments: Comprehensive ferroptosis resistance mechanistic study, though the relative contribution of iron depletion vs. NRF2-driven antioxidant responses to RSL3 resistance remains somewhat ambiguous.

model_io.model: Osteosarcoma cell lines (U2OS, MG-63, HOS, Saos2, 143B)

hypotheses.hypothesis: GPX4 inhibition activates compensatory NRF2-driven antioxidant and iron homeostasis pathways that confer ferroptosis resistance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: RSL3 treatment upregulates AKR1C1, HO-1, NQO1, ferritin, ferroportin; combination targeting of survival pathways synergizes with RSL3

nature:10.1038/s41418-026-01723-8.task: Uncover DR5/NF-κB feedback loop driving sunitinib resistance in renal cell carcinoma
nature:10.1038/s41418-026-01723-8.key_results: DR5 upregulation in resistant ccRCC; DR5 enhances p65 stabilization via competitive WDR12 binding; positive DR5-p65-BCL2 feedback loop drives TKI resistance
nature:10.1038/s41418-026-01723-8.comments: Clear mechanistic feedback loop with validated therapeutic target combination; clinical translation requires demonstrating target engagement in patient tumors.

model_io.model: ccRCC cell lines and xenografts; patient tissues

hypotheses.hypothesis: DR5 upregulation promotes sunitinib resistance by enhancing NF-κB signaling through p65 stabilization via WDR12-CUL4B competition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: DR5 gain-of-function increases p65 stability; DR5 loss-of-function sensitizes to sunitinib; high DR5 predicts poor TKI response in patients

nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08587-3.task: Elucidate synaptic α2δ4 loss mechanism in photoreceptor degeneration
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08587-3.key_results: α2δ4 loss causes delayed photoreceptor degeneration (~7 months) with reduced synaptic mitochondria and TCA cycle downregulation; disrupted calcium crosstalk between plasma membrane, ER, mitochondria
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08587-3.comments: Comprehensive mechanistic study bridging early synaptic dysfunction to late cell death, though the specific downstream effectors connecting calcium dysregulation to ferroptosis-like pathways need clarification.

model_io.model: α2δ4 KO mice retinas; HK-2 cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: α2δ4 loss impairs Cav1.4-mediated calcium signaling, disrupting mitochondrial calcium handling and metabolic homeostasis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: reduced RyR2 and MCU in synaptic terminals; TCA cycle downregulation precedes degeneration

nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08593-5.task: Overcome PI3Kδ inhibitor resistance in follicular lymphoma
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08593-5.key_results: Linperlisib + chiglitazar combination achieves superior tumor control vs. monotherapy; FoxO1 reactivation is mechanistic driver and biomarker
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08593-5.comments: Strong preclinical rationale with clear biomarker, though clinical translation remains to be demonstrated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: PI3Kδ and PPARα dual targeting reactivates FoxO1 to promote apoptosis in FL
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: FoxO1 genetic depletion attenuates treatment response; FoxO1 identified as pharmacodynamic biomarker

nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08604-5.task: Link DNA damage response protein IFI16 to ferroptosis in acute kidney injury
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08604-5.key_results: IFI16/p204 elevated in AKI; tubule-specific p204 knockout ameliorates ferroptosis; IFI16 enhances PARP-1 PARylation to activate ATM-p53 signaling driving ferroptosis
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08604-5.comments: Novel mechanistic link between DDR sensor IFI16 and ferroptosis, though the downstream ferroptotic executors (e.g., ferritinophagy vs. lipid peroxidation pathways) could be more thoroughly dissected.

model_io.model: Mouse renal I/R injury model; HK-2 renal tubular epithelial cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: IFI16 enhances PARP-1-mediated PARylation to activate ATM-p53 signaling, promoting ferroptotic cell death in AKI
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: p204 deficiency/IFI16 knockout reduces ferroptosis in I/R-AKI; IFI16 binds PARP-1 and potentiates PARylation-dependent DDR

nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08627-y.task: Identify HNF4α isoform-driven metabolic rewiring in gastric cancer metastasis
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08627-y.key_results: P2-HNF4α highly expressed in metastatic lesions; directly activates HKDC1 promoter; mycophenolic acid suppresses HKDC1-driven migration
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08627-y.comments: Clear therapeutic target identification with drug repurposing potential, though the breadth of HNF4α transcriptional targets in this context remains incompletely characterized.

model_io.model: Gastric cancer cell lines and xenografts

hypotheses.hypothesis: P2-HNF4α promotes GC metastasis by transactivating HKDC1 and enhancing glycolysis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: P2-HNF4α binds HKDC1 enhancer; mycophenolic acid suppresses HKDC1 and blocks migration in vitro and in vivo

nature:10.1038/s41422-026-01235-7.task: Determine neuronal mechanism linking social status to adaptive immune responses
nature:10.1038/s41422-026-01235-7.key_results: Second-ranked mice show superior T-cell responses; selective strengthening of dorsomedial prefrontal cortex (dmPFC) synapses or neural activity boosts antigen-specific T-cell percentages
nature:10.1038/s41422-026-01235-7.comments: Striking neuro-immune coupling discovery with clear causal evidence, though the downstream sympathetic/parasympathetic and endocrine pathways remain incompletely mapped.

model_io.model: Social hierarchies in male mice; dmPFC-specific manipulations

hypotheses.hypothesis: dmPFC synaptic strength causally regulates T-cell response magnitude downstream of social status
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: selective dmPFC synapse strengthening or neural activation sufficient to boost antigen-specific T-cell percentages independent of rank

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70734-3.task: Identify chloroplast splicing complex components and mechanisms
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70734-3.key_results: MatK forms heteromultimeric complex with MKIP1 (neofunctionalized branching enzyme); MKIP1 silencing reduces MatK intron target splicing by >50%
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70734-3.comments: Elegant functional divergence story, though the precise molecular mechanism of how MKIP1 facilitates splicing remains somewhat unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: MKIP1 (branching enzyme homologue) facilitates MatK-mediated splicing of group IIA introns
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: MKIP1 co-precipitates all known MatK intron targets; MKIP1 silencing reduces target splicing

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70846-w.task: Uncover sex-linked histone demethylase control of hepatic cholesterol metabolism
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70846-w.key_results: Female-specific KDM6A requirement for hepatic lipid gene expression; KDM6A cooperates with HNF4A to enable CREBH-dependent transcription; female KDM6A-deficient mice develop pro-atherogenic lipoprotein profiles
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70846-w.comments: Well-executed sex-stratified study revealing X-linked epigenetic control of cardiovascular risk, though mechanistic details of how KDM6A demethylation coordinates with HNF4A and CREBH require further definition.

model_io.model: Female and male KDM6A hepatocyte-knockout mice; human hepatocytes

hypotheses.hypothesis: X-linked KDM6A is required for female-specific maintenance of hepatic cholesterol metabolism via HNF4A-CREBH cooperation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: KDM6A depletion in female (but not male) hepatocytes disrupts lipoprotein regulation; female KDM6A-KO mice show pro-atherogenic lipid profiles

nature:10.1038/s41590-026-02473-y.task: Engineer metabolite-sensing receptors to direct NK and T cells to solid tumors
nature:10.1038/s41590-026-02473-y.key_results: GPR183, GPR84, GPR34, GPR18 identified as top infiltration enhancers via CRISPR screens; GPR183 expression in NK/CAR-NK/CAR-T cells increases tumor infiltration and control
nature:10.1038/s41590-026-02473-y.comments: Elegant CRISPR discovery coupled with functional validation; generalizability across tumor types and long-term safety of metabolite-sensing engineered cells warrant investigation.

model_io.model: NK-92 cells (CRISPR screens); engineered NK/CAR-NK/CAR-T cells; mouse breast/ovarian tumor models

hypotheses.hypothesis: Metabolite-sensing GPCRs (particularly GPR183) rewire NK/T-cell migration to enable tumor infiltration and eradication
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: GPR183 expression increases infiltration and tumor control in immunocompetent mice; ligand-dependent transcriptome remodeling observed

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44916-4.task: Define germ cell role in testicular morphogenesis using xenograft model
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44916-4.key_results: Germ cell-containing tissue (10% spermatogonia) develops organized seminiferous tubules; germ cell-free tissue remains fragmented
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44916-4.comments: Well-designed xenograft model with clear transcriptomic support, though mechanistic details of how germ cells direct Sertoli cell organization are limited.

model_io.model: Porcine testicular cell xenografts in immunodeficient mice

hypotheses.hypothesis: Porcine SSCs are indispensable for testicular morphogenesis and tubular architecture
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: germ cell-containing grafts develop well-organized tubules; germ cell-free grafts fail tubule formation

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45054-7.task: Enhance decitabine efficacy in lung cancer via TrxR inhibition
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45054-7.key_results: Butaselen + decitabine combination achieves synergistic cytotoxicity (CI < 1); downregulates DNMT1, reduces Bcl-2/Bax ratio, upregulates p21 and E-cadherin
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45054-7.comments: Mechanistically rational combination with clear synergy metrics, though the breadth of TrxR-dependent pathways underlying the synergy warrants deeper exploration.

hypotheses.hypothesis: TrxR inhibition synergizes with DNMT inhibition to induce apoptosis and suppress migration in lung cancer
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: combination treatment shows enhanced apoptosis and reduced migration vs. single agents; associated with altered epigenetic and apoptotic markers

nature:10.1038/s42003-026-09871-w.task: Assess developmental tolerance to mitotic errors across embryonic stages
nature:10.1038/s42003-026-09871-w.key_results: Pre-gastrula embryos cannot tolerate prolonged CENP-E inhibition; gastrula embryos tolerate hours of inhibition via SAC-mediated mitotic delay
nature:10.1038/s42003-026-09871-w.comments: Innovative optochemical approach reveals developmental stage-dependent SAC functionality, though the leaky SAC mechanism warrants deeper investigation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Spindle assembly checkpoint gradually functionalizes during early development and buffers mitotic errors
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: gastrula SAC mediates mitotic delay that partially corrects misalignment; SAC suppression renders gastrula embryos inviable

nature:10.1038/s42255-026-01476-1.task: Elucidate PLIN5 phosphorylation control of mitochondria-lipid droplet coupling in hepatic lipid handling
nature:10.1038/s42255-026-01476-1.key_results: PLIN5 S155A variant enhances mitochondria-LD contacts and LD expansion; S155E reduces contacts; fasting elevates mitochondria-LD contacts, Western diet attenuates
nature:10.1038/s42255-026-01476-1.comments: Innovative spatial proteomics application revealing nutrient-dependent organelle remodeling; therapeutic potential hinges on achieving cell-type selectivity in vivo.

model_io.model: Hepatocytes (scPhenomics spatial imaging); male mice (fed vs. fasted vs. Western diet)

hypotheses.hypothesis: PLIN5 phosphorylation at S155 regulates mitochondria-LD tethering in a nutrient-dependent manner to control hepatic lipid flux
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: S155A enhances contacts/LD expansion; S155E reduces contacts; fasting increases PLIN5 and mitochondria-LD proximity; S155A overexpression reduces lipotoxicity in WD

nature:10.1038/s41588-026-02552-8.task: Single-cell spatial atlas of human skin anatomy and multicellular neighborhoods
nature:10.1038/s41588-026-02552-8.key_results: 1.2M cells, 45 cell types across 114 samples, 15 anatomic sites; 10 multicellular neighborhoods identified; perivascular neighborhood exhibits pan-disease immune alterations
nature:10.1038/s41588-026-02552-8.comments: Comprehensive organ-wide atlas is landmark; ligand-receptor analysis reveals homeostatic immune-stromal crosstalk with therapeutic relevance.

model_io.model: Single-cell spatial transcriptomics + cell type annotation + neighborhood identification + ligand-receptor analysis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multicellular neighborhoods organize skin anatomy and mediate cell-cell interactions; perivascular neighborhood is conserved across diseases
hypotheses.result: 10 neighborhoods identified; perivascular neighborhood exhibits pan-disease immune alterations and TNF-mediated immune-stromal crosstalk

nature:10.1038/s41591-026-04312-x.task: Diet-microbiome associations and personalized nutrition prediction at species-level resolution
nature:10.1038/s41591-026-04312-x.key_results: Diet predicted 669/724 species (92.4%), 313/320 pathways (97.8%); strong food-microbe links (coffee-Lawsonibacter r=0.43, yogurt-Streptococcus r=0.42); 82.5% longitudinal tracking
nature:10.1038/s41591-026-04312-x.comments: Large cohort (10k) and longitudinal follow-up strengthen associations; causal inference between diet and microbiome remains observational.

model_io.model: Predictive modeling (species/pathway prediction) + feature attribution + dietary intervention simulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Diet significantly predicts microbiome composition, diversity, and function at species-level resolution
hypotheses.result: Diet predicted 92.4% of tested species; food processing degree emerged as key driver; associations persist over 4 years

nature:10.1038/s41592-026-03042-9.task: Expand proteome coverage via unified deep learning model for multiple fragmentation techniques
nature:10.1038/s41592-026-03042-9.key_results: >10% increase in protein identifications across CID, electron-induced, and photodissociation techniques
nature:10.1038/s41592-026-03042-9.comments: Prosit model integration into FragPipe is practical; superior sequence coverage from alternative fragmentation is promising but requires method validation.

model_io.model: Prosit deep learning model (unified spectrum prediction across fragmentation methods)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unified deep learning model trained on multi-enzyme datasets generalizes across collision-, electron-, and photon-based fragmentation
hypotheses.result: Electron-induced and UV photodissociation achieve competitive identification efficiency with superior coverage vs. CID

nature:10.1038/s41598-025-30521-4.task: Causal relationships between CSF/plasma metabolites and Parkinson's disease via Mendelian randomization
nature:10.1038/s41598-025-30521-4.key_results: 6 CSF metabolites with suggestive causal relationships (dimethylglycine, gluconate, oxalate positive; P-16:0/20:4, X-23587 negative); 49 plasma metabolites; O-sulfo-L-tyrosine most significant protective metabolite
nature:10.1038/s41598-025-30521-4.comments: MR leverages genetic instruments for causal inference; multiple testing across 1738 metabolites raises multiplicity concerns despite suggestive threshold.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Specific metabolite levels have causal relationships with PD risk
hypotheses.result: O-sulfo-L-tyrosine emerged as most protective plasma metabolite; 6 CSF metabolites with suggestive causal associations

nature:10.1038/s41598-025-34733-6.task: Compressed deepfake detection via GA-LASSO feature selection and lightweight classifiers
nature:10.1038/s41598-025-34733-6.key_results: >90% feature reduction (2048→120–170); AUC 99.48% (FF++ C23); robust under compression and cross-dataset testing
nature:10.1038/s41598-025-34733-6.comments: Feature reduction is substantial; single-seed results are strong but multi-seed analysis reveals variance, particularly under heavy compression.

model_io.model: GA-LASSO feature selection on ResNet50 embeddings + KNN/SVM classifiers

hypotheses.hypothesis: GA-LASSO reduces ResNet50 features while maintaining discriminative power for deepfake detection
hypotheses.result: >90% feature reduction with competitive AUC (99.48% FF++ C23); robust generalization under compression

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-41007-2.task: Carotid artery lumen segmentation via YOLOv11n and active contour models
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-41007-2.key_results: Longitudinal: Dice 94.9%, accuracy 97.7%; transverse: Dice 90.8%, accuracy 99.6%; near real-time inference (<1 s)
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-41007-2.comments: Hybrid pipeline is practical for clinical use; robust to low US image quality, though evaluation limited to unlisted datasets.

model_io.model: YOLOv11n object detection + multistage preprocessing + Chan-Vese level-set segmentation

hypotheses.hypothesis: YOLOv11n localization followed by contour-based segmentation improves lumen delineation under challenging US conditions
hypotheses.result: Hybrid pipeline achieved Dice >90% on both sections; numerical stability depends critically on YOLOv11n localization

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-41998-y.task: Diabetic retinopathy detection from retinal fundus images
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-41998-y.key_results: 94.6% accuracy, F1-score 0.94, AUC-ROC 0.96 on EyePACS dataset
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-41998-y.comments: Physics-aware feature selection via DGOA is novel, but comparative baselines (EfficientNetV2S, MGA-CSG) lack published citations in abstract.

model_io.model: Dynamic Grasshopper Optimization Algorithm (DGOA) + Ensemble Classifier

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive feature selection via DGOA improves DR detection by exploiting physically meaningful image patterns
hypotheses.result: Ensemble model achieved superior accuracy and generalization vs. single-model approaches

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43762-8.task: Sequential behavior modeling for digital customer journeys via LDA/HLDA and Markov chains
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43762-8.key_results: LDA/hLDA identified customer journey archetypes; Markov chains captured behavioral sequences; framework detects irregularities and predicts undesirable outcomes in HMO app (120k users, 5.5M events/day)
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43762-8.comments: Novel integration of topic modeling and Markov chains for customer journey; real-world HMO dataset is valuable but domain-specific.

model_io.model: Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA/hLDA) + session clustering + Markov chain modeling
inputs: event logs from HMO mobile app (120k users)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Topic-based session summarization reduces customer journey complexity and enables detection of irregular behavioral patterns
hypotheses.result: Framework identified distinct customer archetypes and flagged high-risk behavioral sequences for intervention

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44326-6.task: Bioinformatic functional analysis of myostatin gene polymorphisms in Ukrainian Carpathian sheep
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44326-6.key_results: 8 known + 1 novel SNP identified in MSTN intron 1; c.373+607G>A and c.373+283T>C flagged as candidates for functional investigation
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44326-6.comments: Novel polymorphism identification is valuable; functional effects remain predicted via in silico approaches without experimental validation.

model_io.model: DNA sequencing + in silico prediction (pre-mRNA stability, transcription factor binding, pre-miRNA formation) + MD simulations

hypotheses.hypothesis: Intronic MSTN polymorphisms affect pre-mRNA stability and transcription factor binding, influencing myostatin expression
hypotheses.result: c.373+607G>A and novel c.373+283T>C identified as functional candidates via in silico prediction

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44591-5.task: Multi-label chest X-ray classification with dual-branch multi-scale attention
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44591-5.key_results: Macro-average AUC 0.847 on ChestX-ray14; dual-branch + cross-attention + PMS-CSA module balances global and local features
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44591-5.comments: Attention mechanisms are well-motivated for multi-label tasks; class imbalance handling via adjusted loss is standard but effective.

model_io.model: CR-MSNet (CoAtNet-2 global branch + ResNet local branch + cross-attention + PMS-CSA module)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dual-branch architecture with cross-attention and multi-scale channel-spatial attention improves multi-label classification
hypotheses.result: CR-MSNet achieved macro-average AUC 0.847, outperforming single-branch methods

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44782-0.task: Action recognition in freestyle wrestling via CNN–Bi-LSTM with foreground segmentation
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44782-0.key_results: 82.9% top-1 accuracy; DeepLabV3+ foreground segmentation improves recognition on high-occlusion techniques
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44782-0.comments: Domain-specific approach mitigates overfitting via transfer learning; modest dataset (210 clips) limits generalization claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Foreground-aware segmentation improves action recognition under occlusion
hypotheses.result: Foreground segmentation + EfficientNet-B7 + Bi-LSTM achieved 82.9% accuracy with consistent gains on high-occlusion techniques

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45190-0.task: Comparative structural and bioinformatic characterization of chalcone synthase across medicinal plants
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45190-0.key_results: Conserved thiolase-fold across 13 species; species-specific substrate-binding channel variations; stable CHS–ligand complexes in MD simulations
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45190-0.comments: Purely in silico; lacks experimental validation, but provides structural hypotheses for future studies.

model_io.model: Sequence alignment (ClustalW) + AlphaFold structure prediction + molecular docking + MD simulations (100 ns) + MM-PBSA
inputs: CHS protein sequences from 13 medicinal plant species + A. thaliana

hypotheses.hypothesis: CHS structural variations across species reflect substrate selectivity rather than functional divergence
hypotheses.result: AlphaFold structures conserve catalytic triad and key motifs; species-specific substrate-binding channel variations identified

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45456-7.task: Identify genetic variants and key genes associated with chronic lymphocytic leukemia
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45456-7.key_results: 2357 significant SNPs at p<5×10⁻⁸; 40 genomic loci; 7 key genes (TERT, BCL2L11, FAS, BCL2, CFLAR, CASP8, LEF1); 4 candidate drugs identified
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45456-7.comments: GWAS meta-analysis is comprehensive; functional validation and drug prediction remain in silico.

model_io.model: METAL (SNP meta-analysis) + FUMA (LD-based annotation) + PPI network analysis + drug-gene interaction mapping

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-GWAS integration and functional mapping identify CLL risk loci and druggable key genes
hypotheses.result: 7 key genes identified with known drug interactions; 4 candidate therapeutics proposed

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45486-1.task: Automated educational question classification via Bloom's Taxonomy using RoBERTa ensemble
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45486-1.key_results: 92.37% accuracy, macro F1-score 0.923, AUC 0.992; weighted ensemble outperforms GRU/LSTM/CNN individual models (p=0.048)
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45486-1.comments: Ensemble methodology is standard; statistical significance confirmed via McNemar's test, though marginal improvement over best single model.

hf:2603.08462.task: Efficient chain-of-thought reasoning with computational budget constraints

hypotheses.hypothesis: AI agents and human reviewers provide different types of feedback

hypotheses.result: AI suggestions adopted at significantly lower rate; over half of unadopted AI suggestions are incorrect or address via alternative fixes; adopted AI suggestions cause larger complexity increases

hypotheses.hypothesis: AI agents can scale defect screening but lack contextual judgment
hypotheses.result: Human oversight remains critical for suggestion quality and contextual feedback

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-37953-6.comments: Novel unsupervised generative approach addresses a genuine computational bottleneck in materials science.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Inverse VAE can efficiently sample configuration space without expensive Monte Carlo or incomplete SQS methods
hypotheses.result: Demonstrated on uranium-plutonium mixed oxides; provides physical insight into defect behavior

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44118-y.task: Predictive energy management in hybrid UAVs via GRNN-DP-MPC
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44118-y.key_results: GRNN-DP-MPC integrates voltage forecasting, dynamic programming, and model predictive control; reduces battery energy consumption and improves endurance under practical conditions
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44118-y.comments: Three-stage pipeline (GRNN prediction → DP global optimization → MPC real-time control) well-designed; simulation and experimental validation provided.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Predictive GRNN-DP-MPC co-optimization reduces battery energy consumption vs. reactive strategies
hypotheses.result: Simulation and experimental validation confirm improved endurance and reduced battery usage

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44297-8.task: Bone tumor segmentation and classification from X-ray images
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44297-8.key_results: 98.5% accuracy, 98.57% F1-score, 97% Dice score on 809 X-ray images; 48 ms inference time on RTX 3090
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44297-8.comments: Strong dataset-specific results but lacks external multi-institutional validation, limiting clinical applicability claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: ROI-restricted relational attention reduces computational complexity while maintaining long-range context
hypotheses.result: 12.3M parameters vs. UNet/Mask R-CNN, maintaining 98.5% accuracy

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44298-7.task: Replica node detection in mobile wireless sensor networks
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44298-7.key_results: KN-MCDP protocol achieves high detection rate, reduces storage/communication overhead, balances energy, extends network lifetime; multi-point collaboration with key negotiation prevents information leakage
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44298-7.comments: Extends traditional static-WSN detection to mobile WSNs; encryption (digital signature, ternary polynomial, symmetric) and Bloom filters reduce overhead; practical energy-aware design.

model_io.model: KN-MCDP protocol (cluster heads + mobile nodes + base station)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-point collaboration with encrypted information exchange detects replicas in both static and mobile WSN nodes while preserving energy
hypotheses.result: High detection rate; reduced storage/communication overhead; extended network lifetime

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44523-3.task: Markerless monocular distance measurement for standing long jump
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44523-3.key_results: Vision pipeline achieves 0.71 cm MAE on field tests; ~23 FPS, ~42.7 ms per frame; ablations show 25.07 cm error without athlete handling, 4.35 cm without perspective mapping
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44523-3.comments: Practical system demonstrating centimeter-level accuracy; key-frame selection, homography, heel segmentation critical; minimal calibration enables school deployment.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Homography-based plane mapping with polynomial perspective-edge bias correction enables centimeter-level distance accuracy
hypotheses.result: 0.71 cm MAE; 4.35 cm error degradation without perspective mapping

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44544-y.task: Predict graphene-based patch antenna performance for THz applications
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44544-y.key_results: ANN predicts antenna performance (S11, VSWR, gain, efficiency, radiation pattern) with R² = 0.99 in 0.7 ms; trained on 784 CST simulations
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44544-y.comments: ANN outperforms RF and SVM as surrogate model, dramatically reducing simulation time; enables rapid design exploration in THz antenna space.

inputs: patch length Lp, width Wp; graphene chemical potential μc, relaxation time τ
outputs: S11, VSWR, gain, efficiency, radiation pattern

hypotheses.hypothesis: ANN can learn nonlinear relationships between antenna geometry, graphene properties, and EM responses
hypotheses.result: ANN achieves R² = 0.99 for all outputs; 1000× speedup vs. full EM simulation

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44686-z.task: V2V task offloading in vehicular edge computing
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44686-z.key_results: Performance-optimized framework achieves 27% system delay improvement, 18% packet delivery ratio gain, 24% task completion ratio increase vs. baselines; cumulative-score dynamic priority queuing manages high density/mobility
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44686-z.comments: Two-stage design (context-aware edge selection + priority queuing) pragmatic; tunable priority weights adapt to application requirements; OMNeT++/Veins/SUMO validation.

inputs: vehicle topology (speed, distance), task properties (size, priority)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cumulative score weighting (relative speed, distance, task size, priority) improves task completion vs. simple distance-based offloading
hypotheses.result: 27% system delay reduction, 24% task completion gain under high density/mobility

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44919-1.task: Predict student dropout risk in technical education
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44919-1.key_results: ML models identify at-risk students from 99,104 Moodle activity records (154 students); logistic regression and decision tree classifiers enable early intervention
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44919-1.comments: Weighted attribute method integrating engagement, difficulty, time allocation is sensible; limited quantitative performance metrics reported in abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Weighted aggregation of behavioral and cognitive metrics predicts dropout risk better than individual signals

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44941-3.task: Mine maximal fault-tolerant frequent itemsets in noisy data
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44941-3.key_results: Pattern-growth FT-FP-tree framework substantially reduces execution time vs. existing Apriori-based algorithms; improved memory efficiency vs. other pattern-growth methods; single-pass tolerance evaluation
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44941-3.comments: First pattern-growth approach for FT-MFI mining; transaction mapping and conditional pattern extraction enhance scalability; addresses exponential candidate growth limitation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pattern-growth on FT-FP-tree with single-pass tolerance evaluation scales better than Apriori-based candidate generation
hypotheses.result: Substantial execution time reduction and improved memory efficiency on benchmark datasets

hf:2603.19987.task: Breaking the capability ceiling of LLM post-training via Markov states
hf:2603.19987.key_results: Markov state formulation reduces sample complexity and consistently improves performance on complex logic puzzles beyond standard RL post-training baselines
hf:2603.19987.comments: Addresses a fundamental bottleneck in LLM RL by revisiting classical RL principles; the theoretical guarantees and empirical validation on structured reasoning tasks are compelling.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicit Markov states reduce sample complexity compared to history-as-state formulations
hypotheses.result: Theoretical guarantees provided; empirically validated on logic puzzles

hypotheses.hypothesis: Markov states enable novel reasoning discovery beyond pre-trained patterns
hypotheses.result: Consistent performance improvements across multiple complex tasks

hf:2603.20193.task: Pixel-level image tampering detection and semantic classification
hf:2603.20193.key_results: Per-pixel tampering maps with semantic supervision reveal substantial scoring differences in mask-only baselines; new benchmark and metrics quantify localization confidence and tamper meaning understanding
hf:2603.20193.comments: Shifts evaluation from coarse region masks to fine-grained pixel-level semantics, exposing significant limitations of prior work and establishing rigorous standards.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mask-only metrics substantially over- and under-score tampering detection
hypotheses.result: Re-evaluation of segmentation baselines reveals significant discrepancies and failure modes on micro-edits

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pixel-grounded, meaning-aware evaluation improves detection quality assessment
hypotheses.result: New benchmark with paired supervision enables unified detection and classification protocol

hf:2603.19470.task: Off-policy correction for stable LLM RL via adaptive layerwise perturbation
hf:2603.19470.key_results: ALP prevents importance ratio blow-up and KL spikes during training; improves performance on math and multi-turn tool-integrated reasoning with boosted exploration
hf:2603.19470.comments: Elegant solution to off-policy instability by injecting learnable perturbations at intermediate representations; representation-level perturbations outperform logits-only variants.

model_io.model: LLM with Adaptive Layerwise Perturbation (ALP) in RL training

hypotheses.hypothesis: Layerwise perturbations reduce importance ratio tail and flatten policy distribution
hypotheses.result: Empirically validated: importance ratio tail reduction and eliminated KL spikes

hypotheses.hypothesis: Representation-level perturbations are more effective than logits-only perturbations
hypotheses.result: Ablations confirm all-layer variants substantially outperform partial-layer and logits-only baselines

hf:2603.18750.task: Comparative evaluation of neural detectors for AI-generated text
hf:2603.18750.key_results: Supervised neural detectors (MLP, CNN, MobileNet, Transformer) achieve more stable and robust performance than commercial tools (ZeroGPT, GPTZero, etc.) across languages and domains
hf:2603.18750.comments: Systematic comparison across diverse architectures and commercial detectors reveals superiority of supervised approaches; evaluation spans multiple languages and thematic domains.

model_io.model: Four neural architectures: MLP, 1D-CNN, MobileNet-CNN, Transformer

hypotheses.hypothesis: Supervised neural detectors outperform commercial tools on consistency and robustness
hypotheses.result: Superior performance validated on COLING Multilingual Dataset (English, Italian) and custom Art/Mental Health datasets

hypotheses.hypothesis: Different architectures exhibit varying effectiveness across domains
hypotheses.result: Comparative analysis reveals architecture-dependent strengths and limitations

hf:2603.18655.task: Semi-supervised ultrasound image segmentation with multiscale and frequency-domain learning
hf:2603.18655.key_results: At 5% labeling ratio: 80.04% Dice on LN-INT, 85.52% Dice on DDTI, 83.48% Dice on Prostate; semi-supervised approach exceeds fully supervised baselines with 1.8M parameters
hf:2603.18655.comments: Clever dual innovation (spatial multiscale mixing and frequency-domain contrastive learning) effectively addresses data scarcity and ultrasound imaging artifacts; parameter efficiency is notable.

model_io.model: Teacher-student SSL framework with Multiscale Switch (MSS) and Frequency Domain Switch (FDS)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hierarchical patch mixing (MSS) achieves uniform spatial coverage for SSL
hypotheses.result: Consistent improvements across six diverse ultrasound datasets

hypotheses.hypothesis: Frequency-domain contrastive learning provides robust feature representations under speckle noise
hypotheses.result: Semi-supervised approach exceeds fully supervised baselines despite limited labeled data

nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08634-z.task: Identify ferroptosis mechanism in age-related heart failure
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08634-z.key_results: SIRT1 deficiency promotes cardiac ferroptosis via GATA4-HADHA-GPX4 axis; GPX4 overexpression and ferroptosis inhibition restore cardiac function in aged mice
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08634-z.comments: Strong mechanistic chain from aging regulator to ferroptosis executors, though therapeutic translation requires addressing SIRT1's pleiotropic effects.

model_io.model: Aged rats and D-galactose-induced aging mice

hypotheses.hypothesis: SIRT1 regulates HADHA transcription via GATA4 to suppress ferroptosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SIRT1 overexpression restores HADHA and protects cardiac function

hypotheses.hypothesis: HADHA loss causes mitochondrial dysfunction and glutathione depletion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; HADHA knockdown recapitulates ferroptotic cardiac remodeling

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70934-x.task: Establish pentose phosphate pathway as metabolic checkpoint for cDC antitumor immunity
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70934-x.key_results: PPP augmentation in intratumoral cDCs boosts antitumor responses; PPP drives purine metabolism increasing ATP/GTP for cGAS-STING signaling; combination with PD-L1 blockade synergistic; PPP enrichment correlates with STING activity in patient breast tumors
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70934-x.comments: Identifies metabolic lever for immune checkpoint therapy; elegant mechanistic link through nucleotide biosynthesis; clinical translation potential via small-molecule PPP activators.

model_io.model: Tumor-bearing mice and patient breast cancer samples

hypotheses.hypothesis: PPP augmentation enhances purine availability for cGAMP synthesis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; PPP inhibition reduces ATP/GTP and cGAMP

hypotheses.hypothesis: PPP potentiation + PD-L1 blockade synergistically enhance immunotherapy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in combination studies

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44120-4.task: Characterize disease-stage-specific GAG sulfation changes in COPD lung tissue
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44120-4.key_results: Total GAG increased in GOLD II-IV; CS/DS 4-O-sulfation enhanced across stages; HS 2-O-sulfation elevated in GOLD IV; CHST11 upregulated via TGF-β signaling; TGF-β stimulation of fibroblasts recapitulates changes

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44595-1.task: Characterize L-type VGCC role in F11 neuronal cell differentiation and oxidative stress
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44595-1.key_results: L-type VGCC upregulation during differentiation; CaV1.3 overexpression enhances neurite outgrowth but increases oxidative stress and impairs differentiation; CaV1.3 more deleterious than CaV1.2 due to lower activation threshold

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45586-y.task: Characterize transcriptional programs underlying dual BRAF/MEK inhibitor resistance in melanoma

nature:10.1038/s41564-026-02274-x.task: Climate-driven increase in soil antibiotic resistance
nature:10.1038/s41564-026-02274-x.key_results: Drought correlates with elevated antibiotic-producing bacteria metagenomic signatures across diverse soil types; drought concentrates natural antibiotics, selecting for resistance; hospital antibiotic resistance correlated with aridity index across 116 countries (controlling for income)
nature:10.1038/s41564-026-02274-x.comments: Establishes previously underrecognized link between environmental stress (drought) and antibiotic resistance at global scale; suggests climate-mediated resistance selection as overlooked public health threat.

outputs: antibiotic concentration; resistant vs. sensitive strain abundance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Drought concentrates soil antibiotics, selecting for resistance in microbial populations
hypotheses.result: Metagenomic signatures of antibiotic producers enriched; experimental evidence of concentration-driven selection

hypotheses.hypothesis: Local aridity predicts hospital antibiotic resistance at global scale

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cohort hybrid imputation + global quantile normalization improves cross-cohort protein expression comparability

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44979-3.task: Diagnosis and recurrence prediction of odontogenic keratocyst (OKC)

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70882-6.task: Elucidate coupling between octahedral tilting and B-site off-centering in halide perovskites

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70950-x.task: Achieve ultrasensitive photodetection via pressure-induced atomic-level optimization of graphene-GO heterojunctions

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-42808-1.task: Reduce noise in eddy current measurements for substrate conductivity under coatings
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-42808-1.key_results: Taguchi optimization of self-differential probe parameters reduces noise; rectangular coil design achieves maximum SNR; variance analysis ranks parameter influences; Monte Carlo validation confirms advantages
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-42808-1.comments: Practical optimization study with solid experimental validation, though the novelty is primarily in parameter tuning rather than fundamental methodology.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Optimized rectangular coil geometry maximizes SNR for substrate conductivity measurement
hypotheses.result: Rectangular coil design selected as optimal among investigated alternatives

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44713-z.task: Explain Hubble tension via fractal information growth in black holes
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44713-z.key_results: Two fractal growth processes yield H₀ = 62.79 ± 4.56 km/s/Mpc (early universe) and 70.07 ± 0.39 km/s/Mpc (late universe), matching observed tension
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44713-z.comments: While the numerical coincidence is striking, the physical mechanism linking black hole entropy to cosmological expansion requires further justification and independent verification.

outputs: Hubble constant estimates (early and late universe)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hubble tension arises from intrinsic fractal dynamics in early versus late universe
hypotheses.result: Computed H₀ values match observational discrepancy without invoking large-scale structure anomalies

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70539-4.task: Identify transcriptional network rewiring driving lineage-specific virulence differences in M. tuberculosis
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70539-4.key_results: LysG induction promotes bedaquiline tolerance in L2 strains; DosR regulon shows 2-3× higher basal expression and stronger nitric oxide response in hyper-virulent L2 versus low-virulence L1
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70539-4.comments: The linking of a single master regulator (LysG) to a clinically relevant drug phenotype is elegant, though causality relies on induction experiments rather than genetic knockout in clinical strains.

outputs: DosR and LysG regulon expression levels

hypotheses.hypothesis: Limited genetic variation in MTBC yields major phenotypic differences through differential transcriptional regulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: one in three SNPs affected gene expression; transcript-protein correlations were lineage and condition-specific

hypotheses.hypothesis: LysG promotes reduced metabolic activity and bedaquiline tolerance in L2
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by time-course induction experiments under hypoxia and reaeration

hypotheses.result: Confirmed; arginine restriction and DDX3X inhibition suppress metastasis

nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08623-2.task: Define ME2's role in mitophagy suppression via ATAD3A stabilization
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08623-2.key_results: ME2 depletion enhances PINK1-Parkin mitophagy; ME2 competes with E3 ligase TRIM25 for ATAD3A binding; ATAD3A ubiquitination and degradation increase PINK1 levels; hyperactivated mitophagy suppresses hepatoma proliferation
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08623-2.comments: This ME2-ATAD3A-PINK1 axis links metabolic enzyme function to mitochondrial quality control; metabolic-autophagy crosstalk is emerging as a cancer vulnerability.

model_io.model: Hepatoma cell lines with ME2 knockdown/pharmacological mitophagy inhibition

hypotheses.hypothesis: ME2 suppresses mitophagy by stabilizing ATAD3A through competitive TRIM25 inhibition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ME2 knockdown enhances mitophagy and reduces proliferation

nature:10.1038/s41420-026-03042-w.task: Determine Tempol's iron-limiting mechanism and effects on iron-sulfur cluster metabolism
nature:10.1038/s41420-026-03042-w.key_results: Tempol limits iron bioavailability via ascorbate-iron redox balance, triggering IRP2/HIF1α upregulation and partial ISC synthesis suppression; inhibits ferroptosis
nature:10.1038/s41420-026-03042-w.comments: The distinction from iron chelators and reliance on redox cycling rather than direct chelation is mechanistically important for understanding iron homeostasis.

model_io.model: Multiple cell lines with varying ISC protein dependencies

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ascorbate/iron redox balance is essential for ISC homeostasis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ascorbate reverses Tempol effects on ISC proteins

nature:10.1038/s41420-026-03052-8.task: Establish ATP6V1B2's role in hepatic lipid metabolism via lysosomal acidification
nature:10.1038/s41420-026-03052-8.key_results: ATP6V1B2 downregulation increases lipid accumulation and FASN upregulation; ATP6V1B2 promotes lysosomal FASN degradation; ATP6V1B2 is reduced in MASLD patient serum
nature:10.1038/s41420-026-03052-8.comments: V-ATPase subunit mutations present a tractable metabolic target for fatty liver disease without directly targeting lipogenic enzymes.

model_io.model: Hepatocyte cell lines and MASLD patient samples

hypotheses.hypothesis: ATP6V1B2 maintains lysosomal acidification required for FASN degradation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ATP6V1B2 loss impairs FASN lysosomal catabolism

nature:10.1038/s41421-026-00876-7.key_results: Bilirubin directly binds RIPK3 residues D161/S102, triggering MLKL activation without RIPK1 or RHIM domains; Ripk3 depletion reduces bilirubin neurotoxicity in mice
nature:10.1038/s41421-026-00876-7.comments: This non-canonical activation pathway bypasses traditional death receptor signaling, revealing a metabolic trigger for necroptosis.

model_io.model: Cell lines and murine neurodegeneration models

hypotheses.hypothesis: Bilirubin-RIPK3 interaction is RIPK1 and RHIM-independent
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; necroptosis occurs without canonical pathway components

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70410-6.task: Identify CDK9 as a cardiac inflammation driver via NF-κB phosphorylation
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70410-6.key_results: CDK9 T186A mutation attenuates Ang II-induced heart remodeling; CDK9 directly phosphorylates NF-κB p65 independent of IKKβ and RNAPII pathways
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70410-6.comments: Cell cycle-independent CDK9 function in terminally differentiated cardiomyocytes is noteworthy; therapeutic inhibition was validated in mouse models.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CDK9 Thr-186 phosphorylation is required for direct p65 binding and activation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; T186A blocks inflammation, T186E enhances it

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70516-x.task: Characterize transient molecular chimerism in kleptoplast photosynthesis
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70516-x.key_results: Nuclear-encoded RvRbcS-like and RuBisCO activase support photosynthesis in acquired Tetraselmis kleptoplasts; RvRbcS-like has carboxyl-terminal extension for pyrenoid reorganization; knockdown/knockout impair photosynthesis
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70516-x.comments: This represents a naturally occurring transient xenogeneic organelle system distinct from constitutive chimerism; de novo transport system assembly after acquisition is mechanistically unique.

model_io.model: Rapaza viridis flagellate with kleptoplast acquisition from Tetraselmis

hypotheses.hypothesis: RvRbcS-like protein supports both photosynthesis and kleptoplast remodeling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; knockdown/knockout impair both processes

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70763-y.task: Elucidate tuft cell signaling in airway remodeling via cysteinyl leukotriene-OXGR1-SOX9 axis
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70763-y.key_results: Tuft cells generate CysLTs activating OXGR1 on SOX9+ gland progenitors; SOX9 deletion prevents remodeling and restores epithelial architecture
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70763-y.comments: This tuft cell-immune circuit represents a critical regulatory node; the finding has translational relevance for human sinus mucosa pathology.

model_io.model: Murine airway injury models with fate mapping and null strains

hypotheses.hypothesis: SOX9 is required for aberrant repair and remodeling downstream of OXGR1
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SOX9 deletion prevents remodeling despite OXGR1 signaling

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70844-y.task: Dissect Hedgehog and EGFR-Ras pathway roles in Drosophila follicle stem cell tumorigenesis
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70844-y.key_results: Hedgehog activates EMT transcription factor Zfh1 and undifferentiated state; EGFR-Ras induces cell cycle defects via Pointed/E2f1; combined overactivation blocks differentiation and induces tumor-like growth
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70844-y.comments: Single-cell transcriptomics reveals transcriptional convergence; pathway cooperation occurs at differentiation checkpoints rather than proliferation alone.

model_io.model: Drosophila follicle epithelium with pathway overactivation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hedgehog and EGFR-Ras converge transcriptionally to prevent differentiation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; combined activation induces tumor-like phenotype with sustained proliferation

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70939-6.task: Identify mitochondrial metabolic imbalance as root cause of haploid genome diploidization
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70939-6.key_results: Haploid ESCs show reduced respiration, altered redox balance, and NADPH overload; NADPH oxidation stabilizes haploidy; reduced AURORA kinase activation impairs mitotic regulation
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70939-6.comments: This metabolic-karyotypic link reveals how redox homeostasis couples to chromosome stability; counterselection of haploidy appears driven by metabolic constraints.

model_io.model: Mouse haploid embryonic stem cells with metabolic profiling

hypotheses.hypothesis: NADPH overload from reduced respiration triggers diploidization via impaired AURORA activation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; NADPH oxidation stabilizes haploid state

nature:10.1038/s41556-026-01912-0.task: Establish nonsense-mediated mRNA decay's role in telomere protection via Trf1 quality control
nature:10.1038/s41556-026-01912-0.key_results: NMD is essential for survival when TRF2 is absent; NMD degrades aberrant Trf1 mRNA preventing truncated TRF1 production; truncated TRF1 impairs telomere protection
nature:10.1038/s41556-026-01912-0.comments: This reveals an unexpected RNA quality control mechanism safeguarding genome stability in pluripotent cells; RNA-mediated telomere protection is unusual.

model_io.model: Mouse embryonic stem cells with TRF2 deletion and NMD modulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: NMD-mediated Trf1 mRNA quality control compensates for TRF2 loss in pluripotent cells
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; NMD depletion causes telomere damage when TRF2 is absent

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-42753-z.task: Determine additive effects of hypoxia and aspirin on glutamine accumulation in PIK3CA-mutant colorectal cancer
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-42753-z.key_results: Aspirin and hypoxia additively increase intracellular glutamine in PIK3CA-MT cells via enhanced ASCT2 amino acid uptake; V-9302 (ASCT2 inhibitor) with aspirin shows antitumor potential
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-42753-z.comments: Metabolic-microenvironmental interaction reveals tumor-intrinsic vulnerability; glutamine transporter targeting synergizes with aspirin therapy.

model_io.model: PIK3CA-WT/MT isogenic SW48 cells and HCT116 cells with hypoxia and aspirin

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hypoxia and aspirin synergistically increase glutamine via ASCT2-dependent uptake in PIK3CA-MT cells
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; V-9302 reverses accumulation and enhances aspirin efficacy

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44822-9.task: Evaluate ambrisentan protection against cisplatin-induced cardiotoxicity via p53/NF-κB regulation
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44822-9.key_results: Ambrisentan reduces cisplatin-induced apoptosis (↓caspase-3/7, ↑Bcl-2), inflammation (↓TNFα/IL6), and mitochondrial damage (↓ROS, promotes fusion, enhances biogenesis); restores mitochondrial function
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44822-9.comments: ETA antagonism provides pleiotropic cardioprotection; mechanistic convergence on p53/NF-κB suggests pathway crosstalk in chemotoxicity.

hypotheses.hypothesis: p53 and NF-κB inhibition enhance ambrisentan's protective effects
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; pharmacological pathway inhibition augments cardioprotection

nature:10.1038/s42003-026-09902-6.task: Clarify DYRK1A dosage and aneuploidy interactions in Down syndrome neuropathology
nature:10.1038/s42003-026-09902-6.key_results: Trisomy 21 aneuploidy induces protein aggregates and neuronal apoptosis; DYRK1A overdosage activates NLRP3 inflammasome in astrocytes; DYRK1A copy number correction plus chemical chaperone rescues apoptosis
nature:10.1038/s42003-026-09902-6.comments: The dual aneuploidy-gene dosage mechanism highlights how therapeutic strategies must address both global cellular stress and specific dosage imbalances.

hypotheses.hypothesis: DYRK1A overdosage and aneuploidy-induced stress synergistically enhance apoptosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; DYRK1A correction plus chaperone rescues neuronal death

nature:10.1038/s10038-026-01472-z.task: Genetic diagnosis of muscular dystrophy-dystroglycanopathy via RNA-seq outlier detection
nature:10.1038/s10038-026-01472-z.key_results: RNA-seq of urine-derived cells detected markedly decreased B3GALNT2 expression (OUTRIDER); genome sequencing identified paternally inherited 357-bp promoter deletion; segregated with phenotype in siblings
nature:10.1038/s10038-026-01472-z.comments: Proof-of-concept that transcriptome-guided outlier analysis can identify non-coding second hits; demonstrates practical diagnostic utility of RNA-seq in genetic disease.

hypotheses.hypothesis: RNA-seq-guided analysis identifies non-coding second pathogenic hits in compound heterozygous variants
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; promoter deletion identified and functionally validated by RNA-seq

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-69988-8.task: Impact of post-pandemic population immunity on zoonotic coronavirus emergence risk
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-69988-8.key_results: Cross-neutralizing antibodies against spike proteins of multiple zoonotic sarbecoviruses present in post-COVID sera; mathematical modeling predicts emergence likelihood of novel sarbecovirus significantly reduced by population cross-immunity; extent depends on cross-protection level and R₀
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-69988-8.comments: Timely analysis of pandemic-generated immunity as barrier to spillover; highlights vaccine strategy implications for future pandemic prevention.

inputs: Sera from individuals with different COVID-19 immunological histories; sarbecovirus S protein sequences
outputs: Cross-neutralizing antibody titers; emergence probability predictions

hypotheses.hypothesis: SARS-CoV-2 circulation and vaccination generate cross-protection against zoonotic sarbecoviruses, reducing emergence likelihood
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; population immunity creates immunological barrier to novel sarbecovirus emergence

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-specificity SARS-CoV-2 vaccine could increase emergence risk by suppressing SARS-CoV-2 prevalence and natural cross-protection
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in simulations; vaccine specificity-emergence tradeoff identified

nature:10.1038/s41591-026-04253-5.task: AI prioritization of chest X-rays in lung cancer diagnostic pathway (RCT)
nature:10.1038/s41591-026-04253-5.key_results: No significant difference in median time to CT with/without AI prioritization (53 vs 53 days, ratio of geometric means 0.97, 95% CI 0.93–1.02, P = 0.31); median time to diagnosis 44 vs 46 days (ratio 0.98, P = 0.84); 30.3% discordance between AI and radiologist
nature:10.1038/s41591-026-04253-5.comments: Null RCT result demonstrates that AI prioritization alone does not accelerate lung cancer diagnosis in UK primary care; questions deployment strategy without systemic workflow integration.

outputs: Prioritization flag; AI report; concordance with radiologist

hypotheses.hypothesis: AI prioritization of CXR findings accelerates time to CT and lung cancer diagnosis
hypotheses.result: Rejected; no significant time difference (geometric mean ratio 0.97, P = 0.31)

nature:10.1038/s41592-026-03030-z.task: Planetary-scale protein clustering across the tree of life
nature:10.1038/s41592-026-03030-z.key_results: 19 billion biosphere proteins aggregated into 544 million nonsingleton clusters; DeepClust database improves AlphaFold2 structure prediction
nature:10.1038/s41592-026-03030-z.comments: Impressive scale and computational efficiency, but specific improvement metrics for structure prediction are not quantified.

outputs: 544 million protein clusters; cluster database for structure prediction enhancement

hypotheses.hypothesis: Clustered protein information from DeepClust can enhance AlphaFold2 predictions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; structure prediction improvements shown

nature:10.1038/s41597-026-07004-w.task: Fluorescence prediction from transmitted light microscopy images
nature:10.1038/s41597-026-07004-w.key_results: Dataset of 56,984 standardized 2D images from 2,574 acquisition sets across 8 imaging centers; supports training generalizable deep learning models for in silico labeling and segmentation
nature:10.1038/s41597-026-07004-w.comments: Large-scale, well-curated dataset with rich metadata should enable robust model development, though actual ML model performance metrics are not provided.

inputs: Transmitted light images (bright-field, phase contrast, DIC)
outputs: Predicted fluorescence images (nucleus, mitochondria, tubulin, actin)

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-41711-z.task: Design and evaluation of pyrimidine-based sulfonamide anticancer agents
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-41711-z.key_results: Compounds 18, 21, 23, 24 show IC50 7.4–10.2 µM vs HepG2 and 8.2–10.0 µM vs MCF-7, approaching 5-fluorouracil potency; compound 23 docking score −5.56 kcal/mol vs thymidylate synthase
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-41711-z.comments: Solid medicinal chemistry work with SAR insights, though in vitro IC50 values alone do not guarantee clinical efficacy or selectivity.

outputs: Binding affinity scores (kcal/mol); H-bond interaction maps

inputs: Synthetic compounds; HepG2, MCF-7, WI-38, VERO cell lines

hypotheses.hypothesis: Electron-donating groups and balanced lipophilicity optimize cytotoxicity and selectivity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by SAR analysis and docking; compounds 18, 21, 23, 24 identified as leads

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-42402-5.task: Length-biased Sujit distribution for modeling clinical remission survival data
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-42402-5.key_results: Length-biased Sujit (LBSJT) distribution derived and characterized; strong parameter estimation accuracy in Monte Carlo simulations, particularly with increasing sample size; superior fit vs established distributions on oncology remission data
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-42402-5.comments: Theoretical statistical contribution with established properties; practical applicability demonstrated but limited to this specific oncology context.

outputs: Distribution parameters; reliability metrics (Bonferroni, Lorenz curves)

model_io.model: Maximum likelihood estimation + Monte Carlo simulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Length-biased Sujit distribution fits clinical remission data better than standard distributions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; LBSJT superior goodness-of-fit in clinical dataset

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43276-3.task: Semi-automated analysis of foraminiferal micro-CT morphology
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43276-3.key_results: ForamJ ImageJ plugin achieves 0.9981 segmentation accuracy (±0.001) and 0.97 Dice score (±0.008) vs manual annotation with 1.30% difference in calcite volume; 154 seconds median processing per sample
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43276-3.comments: Significant advance in reproducibility and throughput for paleontological imaging; validation against commercial software strengthens adoption potential.

outputs: Test segmentation, chamber isolation, morphological metrics (calcite volume, chamber volumes, surface area, wall thickness, centroids)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Semi-automated segmentation and morphological quantification match manual commercial software with high fidelity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Dice score 0.97 ± 0.008, calcite volume difference 1.30% vs Dragonfly

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43578-6.task: Lightweight, interpretable arrhythmia detection with security-aware trust assessment for cardiac IoT
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43578-6.key_results: CLARITY-AI 2.0 achieves F1 = 0.928, AUC = 0.985 (MIT-BIH); generalizes on PTB-XL (AUC 0.940) and Chapman (AUC 0.915); ESP32 deployment footprint 350 KB, latency 8.1 ms
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43578-6.comments: Excellent balance of clinical performance, interpretability, and edge feasibility; intrusion detection and SHAP explanations strengthen clinical trustworthiness.

outputs: Arrhythmia classification (F1, AUC); trust score; attack probability

hypotheses.hypothesis: Intrusion detection + trust scoring improve inference reliability under adversarial network conditions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; flagging unreliable inputs; zero-shot generalization on unseen datasets (AUC 0.915–0.940)

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44199-9.task: Spatiotemporal injury patterns in professional tennis via wearable sensor big data
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44199-9.key_results: Peak injury frequencies during intensive training phases; shoulder-elbow complex 47.3%, lumbar-hip 31.8%; 73.2% injury variance explained by spatiotemporal coupling; Transformer prediction model 91.5% accuracy, AUC 0.956
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44199-9.comments: Comprehensive big data framework for injury epidemiology; high predictive accuracy suggests practical potential for injury prevention systems.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Spatiotemporal coupling of injury distributions and temporal patterns explains injury variance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 73.2% of variance explained by interaction

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44602-5.task: Predicting birdsong syntax from acoustic-sequence rules
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44602-5.key_results: Species rules models predicted syllable sequence accuracy matching tutor songs in both estrildid finch species across colonies; consistent species-specific syntax learned from syllable acoustics and sequence position
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44602-5.comments: Elegant application of NLP-inspired methods to animal behavior; demonstrates that learned syntax follows systematic species-specific rules rather than individual tutoring.

inputs: Syllable acoustic features; sequence position; conspecific song recordings
outputs: Predicted syllable sequences in adult birds

hypotheses.hypothesis: Species rules based on syllable acoustics and sequence position predict syntax as well as tutor songs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in both species; species rules and tutor songs achieved equivalent prediction accuracy

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44617-y.task: Explainable drug sensitivity prediction for breast cancer
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44617-y.key_results: Explainable Drug Graph Attention Transformer (EDrGAT) achieves R² = 0.93, outperforming state-of-the-art models; integrates feature-level graph attention with temporal pharmacodynamics features
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44617-y.comments: Strong performance on drug sensitivity prediction, but lack of validation on independent test cohorts and clinical applicability remain unclear.

inputs: Cell line identity, drug name, drug concentration; temporal pharmacodynamics (lag, rolling mean, exponential mean)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Task-specific graph attention and temporal features improve drug sensitivity prediction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; EDrGAT achieves R² = 0.93 vs prior state-of-the-art

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44640-z.task: In silico evaluation of FDA-approved antivirals against SARS-CoV-2
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44640-z.key_results: Baloxavir Marboxil (BM) highest docking affinity (−8.88 ± 0.57 kcal/mol); Dexamethasone (DM) most stable in MD simulations (RMSD 0.78 ± 0.12 Å); all three drugs show RMSD <3.0 Å confirming complex stability
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44640-z.comments: Computational screening identifies candidates but ADMET concerns (BM/RD hepatotoxicity) and lack of experimental validation limit clinical translation.

inputs: Baloxavir Marboxil, Dexamethasone, Remdesivir; SARS-CoV-2 proteins

hypotheses.hypothesis: BM exhibits highest binding affinity; DM most stable; all show suitable pharmacological profiles
hypotheses.result: Partially confirmed; binding affinities confirmed but ADMET concerns identified for BM/RD

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45080-5.task: Identify druggable genes in liver fibrosis via integrated multi-omics and validation
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45080-5.key_results: Eight upregulated genes (AQP1, CCL19, CXCL6, CXCL9, CXCL10, EPCAM, IGJ, LUM) with good diagnostic performance in liver fibrosis; enriched in immune regulation, ECM remodeling, and inflammatory pathways; AQP1 confirmed by IHC/IF
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45080-5.comments: Systematic integration of public datasets and clinical validation identifies therapeutic candidates, but functional validation of druggability remains preliminary.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Intersection of DEGs, WGCNA modules, and druggable genes identifies therapeutically relevant targets in liver fibrosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; eight genes identified with diagnostic relevance

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45271-0.task: Machine learning for adult age estimation from dental characteristics on radiographs
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45271-0.key_results: Extreme gradient boosting (XGBoost) best ML model: RMSE 10.88 years, R² = 0.70 vs linear regression (RMSE 12.04, R² = 0.63) on 2,415 individuals aged 20–89; sound/missing teeth most influential predictors
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45271-0.comments: Methodological improvement in dental age estimation, but prediction error magnitude (±11 years) limits clinical utility as standalone tool.

model_io.model: Machine learning ensemble (random forest, XGBoost, k-NN, SVM, decision tree, MLP)

hypotheses.hypothesis: ML methods with one-hot encoding preserve dual-feature information better than linear regression
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; XGBoost RMSE 10.88 vs linear regression 12.04 years

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45346-y.task: Temporal machine learning for predicting post-acute sequelae of COVID-19 (PASC)
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45346-y.key_results: TLDR framework achieves AUROC 0.791 vs 0.668 for atemporal baseline (18.4% improvement); PRAUC 0.590 vs 0.421 (40.14% improvement); lower overfitting index (−0.028) on 85,000+ patient EHR data
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45346-y.comments: Demonstrates clear value of temporal sequencing in clinical prediction; generalizability and robustness advantages are noteworthy.

inputs: Time-stamped EHR features; longitudinal patient records
outputs: PASC risk prediction; AUROC, PRAUC scores

hypotheses.hypothesis: Temporal sequencing and dynamic windows improve PASC risk prediction vs atemporal approaches
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; TLDR AUROC 0.791 vs benchmark 0.668

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45396-2.task: Optimize pandemic vaccination strategies using neural networks and optimal control
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45396-2.key_results: Physics-informed neural networks + deep RL vaccination strategy reduces cumulative hospital-days 85.3%, deaths 84.4%, total costs 22.3% vs no vaccination; exceeds actual government rollout by 4–6 percentage points (COVID-19, Victoria)
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45396-2.comments: Demonstrates feasibility of ML for epidemic control optimization, but counterfactual comparison to actual policy limits real-world validation.
