nature:10.1038/s42005-026-02599-3.comments: Classical elastic analogue effectively demonstrates multidimensional adiabatic control, validating shortcuts-to-adiabaticity theory in realistic multiparameter systems.

model_io.model: Coupled elastic waveguides with synthetic parameters

hypotheses.hypothesis: Path optimization enhances robustness against nonadiabatic transitions in 2D parameter spaces
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; restored adiabatic energy transfer observed along optimized trajectory

nature:10.1038/s42254-026-00923-y.task: Review quantum geometry effects on electronic properties in correlated materials
nature:10.1038/s42254-026-00923-y.key_results: Quantum geometry introduces hidden length/timescales via interband mixing and momentum-space wavefunction textures; modifies linear and nonlinear responses and influences many-body ground states
nature:10.1038/s42254-026-00923-y.comments: Perspective article synthesizing emerging understanding of quantum geometric effects across diverse experimental platforms; emphasizes scale separation principles.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quantum geometry qualitatively modifies material responses beyond single-band approximations
hypotheses.result: Reviewed with multiple experimental examples across condensed matter platforms

hf:2603.17198.task: Continual learning without catastrophic forgetting in online data streams
hf:2603.17198.key_results: AAT achieves performance comparable to experience replay baselines while using zero additional memory; eliminates need for replay buffers in strictly online settings
hf:2603.17198.comments: The claim of memory efficiency is valuable, though the evaluation on only two specialized benchmarks (relational masking and proverbs) limits confidence in generalizability to diverse domains.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Structural abstraction can replace experience replay as an inductive bias for continual learning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: AAT matches or exceeds ER baselines without replay buffer

hypotheses.hypothesis: Abstraction helps mitigate catastrophic forgetting in online streams
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: improved stability and generalization without replay

hf:2603.13669.task: Self-supervised no-reference image quality assessment without human labels
hf:2603.13669.key_results: Strong performance on synthetic, authentic, and cross-dataset NR-IQA benchmarks with improved generalization; outperforms contrastive and supervised baselines despite zero human annotations
hf:2603.13669.comments: The compositional distortion engine and dual-source relation graphs are technically novel, but the paper lacks explicit numerical comparisons to recent baselines making performance claims difficult to verify.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Implicit structured relations improve self-supervised quality assessment compared to binary contrastive constraints
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: SHAMISA achieves strong performance without contrastive losses

hypotheses.hypothesis: Compositional distortion engine enables fine-grained representational control
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: structured similarity shifts correlate with distortion severity variations

hf:2603.20576.task: Evaluating AI agents on multi-database natural language data queries
hf:2603.20576.key_results: Best frontier model (Gemini-3-Pro) achieves only 38% pass@1 accuracy; benchmark spans 54 queries across 12 datasets, 9 domains, 4 database systems
hf:2603.20576.comments: This fills an important gap in evaluating end-to-end data agent pipelines, though 38% accuracy suggests the benchmark may be challenging to the point of limited utility for iterative model development.

model_io.model: LLM-based data agent (tested: Gemini-3-Pro, GPT-4, Claude, etc.)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Frontier LLMs can reliably handle multi-database data queries at enterprise scale
hypotheses.result: Rejected: best model achieves only 38% accuracy, revealing significant capability gaps

hypotheses.hypothesis: Failure modes differ systematically across database systems and domains
hypotheses.result: Implicitly confirmed via benchmark design capturing heterogeneous failure scenarios

nature:10.1038/s41586-026-10255-7.task: Determine molecular basis of supercoiling-induced off-target activity in CRISPR–Cas9
nature:10.1038/s41586-026-10255-7.key_results: Negative supercoiling creates DNA structural defects resolved by Cas9 binding; HNH domain adopts more catalytically competent conformation in off-target configuration; topology-dependent structural plasticity in protospacer-adjacent-motif region accommodates DNA–RNA mismatches
nature:10.1038/s41586-026-10255-7.comments: Cryo-EM structures clarify how topological context influences targeting fidelity, but quantitative comparison of on/off-target catalytic rates remains absent.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Negative supercoiling enhances Cas9 off-target activity by inducing DNA structural defects
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; supercoiling-driven defects are relieved upon Cas9 binding

hypotheses.hypothesis: Topology-dependent structural plasticity accommodates mismatches in off-target sites
hypotheses.result: Supported by cryo-EM showing differential protospacer-adjacent-motif plasticity based on DNA topology

nature:10.1038/s41586-026-10276-2.task: Elucidate structural and thermodynamic mechanisms of cold-evoked activation in TRPM8 channels
nature:10.1038/s41586-026-10276-2.key_results: Identified semi-swapped architecture with S6 helix repositioning; cold-evoked stabilization of outer pore region and regulatory lipid binding drives channel opening; pore and TRP helices show greatest stimulus-evoked energetic changes
nature:10.1038/s41586-026-10276-2.comments: Combining cryo-EM with HDX-MS elegantly bridges structural visualization and energetic landscape analysis, though in-cell membrane context validation is limited to two orthologs.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cold-evoked stabilization of the pore region drives gating via S6 helix repositioning and lipid binding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed through cryo-EM structures and HDX-MS energetic mapping

hypotheses.hypothesis: Avian TRPM8 orthologue exhibits cold-insensitivity due to structural differences
hypotheses.result: Supported by structural comparison validating human-specific cold activation mechanism

nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08621-4.task: Define PKM2 dimerization role in osteoarthritis pathogenesis and mitochondrial homeostasis
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08621-4.key_results: PKM2 dimers upregulated in OA cartilage; PKM2 deficiency or tetramer stabilization reduced OA progression via MFN1-dependent mitochondrial fusion; TEPP-46 promoted chondroprotection
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08621-4.comments: Identifies metabolic-mitochondrial axis linking PKM2 oligomerization state to organellar function and cartilage preservation.

model_io.model: mouse DMM OA model; primary chondrocytes

hypotheses.hypothesis: PKM2 dimers activate ERK→MFN1 upregulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed—PKM2-ERK interaction disruption promotes MFN1

hypotheses.hypothesis: MFN1 mediates chondroprotective effects of PKM2 deficiency
hypotheses.result: Confirmed—MFN1 knockdown abrogates protection

nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08651-y.task: Define LRRK2-cGAS-STING-HMGB1 axis in chondrocyte senescence and osteoarthritis progression
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08651-y.key_results: LRRK2 overexpression accelerated chondrocyte senescence and cartilage degeneration in DMM-OA; LRRK2 activated cGAS–STING pathway, increased HMGB1, exacerbated mitochondrial dysfunction; STING inhibitor H-151 partially mitigated senescence
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08651-y.comments: Identifies innate immune-senescence axis linking mitochondrial dysfunction signaling to OA pathogenesis through cGAS-STING.

model_io.model: DMM rat OA model; rAAV-LRRK2; primary chondrocytes

hypotheses.hypothesis: LRRK2 modulates GTPase activity to upregulate HMGB1
hypotheses.result: Confirmed—LRRK2 promotes HMGB1 upregulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: LRRK2 drives senescence via cGAS-STING-HMGB1 axis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed—STING inhibition partially rescues senescence

nature:10.1038/s41536-026-00461-2.task: Characterize molecular and morphological features of axolotl digit regeneration and hedgehog signaling requirement
nature:10.1038/s41536-026-00461-2.key_results: Digit blastemas nerve-dependent with canonical morphogens but minimal A–P patterning gene expression (Shh, Fgf8, Grem1); Hedgehog signaling essential for interphalangeal joint regeneration; activation insufficient alone for fidelity improvement
nature:10.1038/s41536-026-00461-2.comments: Reveals distal outgrowth independent of classical patterning signals; joint regenerative variation not explained by nerve, proliferation, or patterning gene differences—ideal platform for identifying regenerative determinants.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Digit regeneration requires A–P patterning genes (Shh, Fgf8, Grem1)
hypotheses.result: Refuted—minimal patterning gene expression; distal outgrowth independent of signals

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hedgehog signaling sufficient for joint regenerative fidelity
hypotheses.result: Refuted—activation alone insufficient to improve fidelity in robust digits

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45579-x.task: Define c-MYC-mediated BATF3 transcriptional regulation in type 1 diabetes pathogenesis
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45579-x.key_results: c-MYC identified as central regulator of BATF3; c-MYC and BATF3 significantly elevated in T1DM patients (p<0.05); c-MYC binds BATF3 promoter (−1,214 to −1,203 bp); promotes CD8+ T-cell expansion and apoptosis inhibition
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45579-x.comments: Establishes c-MYC–BATF3 axis as mechanistic pathway in pediatric T1DM with biomarker potential.

model_io.model: human PBMCs (T1DM patients n=30, controls n=24); CD8+ T cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: c-MYC is differentially expressed in T1DM and regulates BATF3
hypotheses.result: Confirmed—c-MYC elevated and binds BATF3 promoter

hypotheses.hypothesis: c-MYC-driven BATF3 expression enhances CD8+ T-cell survival
hypotheses.result: Confirmed—promotes proliferation and inhibits apoptosis

nature:10.1038/s42003-026-09878-3.task: Map regulators of FPR1 surface expression and endocytosis in immune cells
nature:10.1038/s42003-026-09878-3.key_results: GRK2/3/6 trigger internalization via parallel β-arrestin-dependent and -independent pathways; mDia1 and ARF6 specifically regulate FPR1 internalization
nature:10.1038/s42003-026-09878-3.comments: Genome-scale CRISPR screens reveal functional redundancy in GRK signaling and identify formin/ARF6 as unexpected endocytosis effectors.

model_io.model: CRISPR/Cas9 screens in cultured cells and primary human neutrophils

hypotheses.hypothesis: GRK6 functions redundantly with GRK2/3 in FPR1 phosphorylation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed—parallel GRK activities trigger internalization

hypotheses.hypothesis: mDia1 and ARF6 regulate β-arrestin-independent FPR1 endocytosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed—both promote non-canonical internalization pathway

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70274-w.task: Fine-tune LLMs for complex iterative clinical diagnostic procedures with evaluation framework
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70274-w.key_results: ClinDiag-GPT outperforms GPT-4o, Claude-3-Haiku, Qwen variants in diagnostic accuracy and procedural performance on 4,421 real-world cases; physician-AI collaboration increases accuracy
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70274-w.comments: Large benchmark dataset (4,421 cases) and multiple baseline comparisons strengthen evaluation; clinical error types warrant detailed analysis.

model_io.model: Fine-tuned LLM (ClinDiag-GPT) trained on clinical diagnostic cases

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fine-tuned LLMs trained on clinical cases outperform general LLMs and baseline models in complex diagnostic workflows
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ClinDiag-GPT outperforms all baseline models; physician-AI collaboration superior to either alone

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70889-z.task: Identify molecular signatures linking CMV infection to immune dysregulation in people living with HIV
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70889-z.key_results: FCRL6 emerges as key biomarker in CMV-seropositive PLHIV; FCRL6 demethylation and upregulation linked to immune activation; Mendelian randomization shows causal relationship
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70889-z.comments: Multi-omics integration (5 layers) is comprehensive; large cohort (1,887) strengthens findings; causality inference strengthens mechanistic understanding.

model_io.model: Multi-omics analysis: immune profiling, transcriptomics, methylomics, proteomics, metabolomics + Mendelian randomization
inputs: Genomics, methylomics, transcriptomics, proteomics, metabolite data from CMV-seropositive/negative PLHIV

hypotheses.hypothesis: CMV seropositivity drives distinct immune activation patterns in PLHIV mediated by FCRL6 upregulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; FCRL6 high expression associated with pro-inflammatory cytokine production and CMV seropositivity; Mendelian randomization supports causality

nature:10.1038/s41540-026-00689-0.task: Stratify HNSCC by metabolic gene expression and develop prognostic risk model
nature:10.1038/s41540-026-00689-0.key_results: Four metabolic subtypes identified; 13-gene model predicts survival better than 47-gene metabolic model; quiescent subtype has longest survival, mixed subtype worst
nature:10.1038/s41540-026-00689-0.comments: Metabolic heterogeneity is well-established; the clinical utility of this specific 13-gene signature requires prospective validation.

model_io.model: Gene expression clustering and Cox regression

hypotheses.hypothesis: OXPHOS and PPP gene expression patterns stratify HNSCC into distinct metabolic subtypes with different clinical outcomes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; mixed metabolic subtype showed worst survival with highest proliferation scores

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-38238-8.task: Machine learning-based primer design and quality assessment for PCR applications
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-38238-8.key_results: SVM and gradient boosting achieve AUC 0.96-0.99 across 10-fold cross-validation; captures complex primer feature interactions; available online tool
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-38238-8.comments: Dataset relatively small (N=316); validation on independent experimental PCR runs would strengthen claims of practical utility.

model_io.model: SVM and gradient boosting classifiers with 16 engineered primer features

hypotheses.hypothesis: Machine learning models capture complex interactions between primer features to predict PCR efficiency better than rule-based methods
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SVM and gradient boosting AUC 0.96-0.99 demonstrate superior performance

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70586-x.task: Multi-core neuromorphic architecture for training spiking neural networks
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70586-x.key_results: 190–330% performance of Jetson Orin; 1.05 TFLOPS/W @ FP16 @ 28nm; 55–85% memory access reduction vs. A100 GPU; successful 20-core training and 5-worker federated learning on FPGA
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70586-x.comments: Hardware-software co-design achieving exceptional energy efficiency; demonstrates scalability from single-core to distributed federated learning, though real-world applicability remains nascent.

model_io.model: Multi-core neuromorphic processor with FP, BP, WG engines
inputs: Spiking neural network weights, spike inputs, backpropagation gradients
outputs: Updated weights, training loss, inference activations

hypotheses.hypothesis: Specialized neuromorphic hardware can train SNNs with dramatically higher energy efficiency than GPUs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed—1.05 TFLOPS/W and >50% memory reduction achieved

nature:10.1038/s41586-026-10265-5.task: End-to-end automation of the scientific research lifecycle
nature:10.1038/s41586-026-10265-5.key_results: The AI Scientist generates research ideas, code, experiments, analysis, manuscripts, and peer review; manuscript passed first-round review at top-tier conference workshop (70% acceptance rate)
nature:10.1038/s41586-026-10265-5.comments: Demonstrates remarkable capability but raises concerns about review system burden and scientific literature quality; acceptance at workshop level rather than main conference suggests limitations.

model_io.model: The AI Scientist (multi-agent foundation model system)
outputs: Research ideas, executable code, experimental results, plots, manuscript, peer reviews

hypotheses.hypothesis: Large foundation models can autonomously navigate the entire scientific process from conception to publication
hypotheses.result: Partially confirmed—achieved workshop-level publication but full journal/main-conference viability unclear

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43310-4.task: Trust propagation and certificate revocation in large-scale IoT networks
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43310-4.key_results: GNN-based framework achieves 89% trust propagation accuracy, 0.904 F1 on revocation decisions; median response latency <5s on networks up to 102,400 devices
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43310-4.comments: Well-motivated combination of GAT for relational dynamics and Bayesian uncertainty; demonstrates scalability, though evaluation is simulation-based rather than on real IoT deployments.

model_io.model: Graph Attention Network + adaptive revocation algorithm
inputs: IoT device topology, trust relationships, behavioral anomaly indicators, device interactions
outputs: Trust embeddings, risk scores for certificate revocation decisions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Graph neural networks can efficiently propagate trust and detect revocation-worthy devices in large IoT networks
hypotheses.result: Confirmed—89% accuracy and sub-5s latency achieved at scale

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45002-5.task: Mini-batch size sensitivity analysis in deep residual networks for load forecasting
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45002-5.key_results: Mini-batch size 64 consistently yields best STLF performance; batch size 32 provides robust alternative; medium-scale batches outperform large/small across climates
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45002-5.comments: Empirical sensitivity analysis with strict experimental controls provides practical guidance; bootstrap significance testing strengthens claims, but mechanistic explanation for why 64 is optimal remains open.

model_io.model: DRN and PCA-DRN with varying mini-batch sizes {8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, ...}
inputs: Electricity load time series, temperature, weather variables

hypotheses.hypothesis: DRN-based STLF performance exhibits pronounced sensitivity to mini-batch size with an optimal medium-range value
hypotheses.result: Confirmed—batch size 64 optimal; 32 robust alternative; performance degrades at extremes

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45718-4.task: Spatial resource allocation for waste management via subtractive clustering
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45718-4.key_results: Proposed road-network geodesic distance-based subtractive clustering solves urban and large-scale allocation; textile waste container optimization case study shows consistency with spatially distributed needs
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45718-4.comments: Practical heuristic-based approach grounded in clustering methodology; geodesic distance incorporation reflects real-world constraints, though quantitative performance metrics are limited.

model_io.model: Modified subtractive clustering with geodesic distances
inputs: Spatial demand distributions, resource capacity constraints, road network topology
outputs: Optimal resource locations and cluster assignments

hypotheses.hypothesis: Road-network-aware subtractive clustering achieves spatial resource distribution matching demand
hypotheses.result: Confirmed—textile waste container placement aligns with spatially distributed needs

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45739-z.task: AI-driven image generation for artistic creation with style control and super-resolution
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45739-z.key_results: StyleDiffusion-HD outperforms baselines on FID, CLIP Score, Style Loss; achieves high subjective aesthetic ratings from experts and public; improves upon FID and style consistency
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45739-z.comments: Well-motivated integration of latent diffusion + style injection + flow-matching SR; evaluation includes both objective metrics and human assessment, strengthening credibility.

model_io.model: StyleDiffusion-HD (LDM + Style Injection Attention + Flow Matching SR)
inputs: Text descriptions, visual style references, artistic concept parameters
outputs: High-resolution generated images with controlled style

hypotheses.hypothesis: Style injection attention and flow-matching SR enable precise bimodal text-visual style control with high fidelity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed—superior FID, CLIP Score, style consistency vs. mainstream models

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45920-4.task: Monitoring psychological state from multimodal physiological signals
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45920-4.key_results: GABT-Net achieves 96.2% accuracy, 0.9431 macro-F1, 0.021 ECE, 99.10% accuracy on highest-confidence samples; outperforms baselines on WESAD dataset
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45920-4.comments: Clever integration of graph attention for instantaneous feature relationships + Bayesian uncertainty quantification; the explicit modeling of collaborative physiological dynamics is well-motivated.

inputs: Multimodal physiological signals (heart rate, EEG, skin conductance, etc.) sampled over time
outputs: Probabilistic predictions of psychological state; uncertainty estimates (ECE)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicit graph modeling of physiological collaboration + Bayesian uncertainty improves psychological state prediction reliability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed—96.2% accuracy and 0.021 ECE demonstrate high reliability

nature:10.1038/s41746-026-02559-9.task: Multi-agent framework for psychiatric diagnosis with clinical reasoning and empathy
nature:10.1038/s41746-026-02559-9.key_results: WiseMind achieves 85.6% top-1 diagnostic accuracy on 1206 simulated conversations + 180 real sessions; outperforms single-agent baselines by 15–54 percentage points; expert review validates clinical soundness and psychological support
nature:10.1038/s41746-026-02559-9.comments: Thoughtful integration of DSM-5 knowledge graph with dual-agent architecture (Reasonable + Emotional Mind); real-world validation (180 human sessions) strengthens claims over pure simulation.

model_io.model: WiseMind (multi-agent with DSM-5 knowledge graph, Reasonable Mind Agent, Emotional Mind Agent)
inputs: Patient symptom reports, demographic data, conversation history
outputs: Diagnostic hypotheses, differential diagnoses, empathetic follow-up questions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Integrating structured clinical reasoning (DSM-5 knowledge) with empathetic communication (dual agents) improves psychiatric diagnostic accuracy and patient trust
hypotheses.result: Confirmed—85.6% accuracy, 15–54% improvement over single-agent LLM baselines; expert validation of clinical soundness

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-69258-7.task: Characterizing unconventional bipartite entanglement in quantum dimer magnet Yb₂Be₂SiO₇ under strong spin-orbit coupling
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-69258-7.key_results: Effective spin-1/2 model describes Yb³⁺ ions; no magnetic order down to 50 mK; anisotropic exchange stabilizes singlet ground state with wavefunction (|↑↑⟩−|↓↓⟩)/√2 or (|↑↑⟩+|↓↓⟩)/√2; magnetization, heat capacity, neutron spectroscopy consistent with isolated dimer model; spin-orbit coupling induces unusual entangled state
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-69258-7.comments: Demonstrates how spin-orbit coupling modifies entanglement structure in dimer systems; direct comparison with standard (|↑↓⟩−|↓↑⟩)/√2 singlet regarding magnetic susceptibility and response functions would clarify physical implications.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Strong spin-orbit coupling in Yb³⁺ dimer magnets stabilizes unconventional singlet entanglement with same-spin character
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via bulk and spectroscopic evidence of anisotropic-exchange-stabilized singlet

nature:10.1038/s41524-026-02048-3.task: Incorporating multipole expansion into equivariant ML potentials for ground and excited-state molecular dynamics
nature:10.1038/s41524-026-02048-3.key_results: Multipole-expanded equivariant potentials accurately capture QM/MM long-range interactions; nonadiabatic excited-state dynamics of ring-opening reaction simulated successfully; transfer learning from environment-free models improves data efficiency; superior prediction of energies/forces vs baseline models
nature:10.1038/s41524-026-02048-3.comments: Elegant integration of physical multipole structure into ML; validated on limited system set (Ni complexes)—generalization to diverse chemical spaces and transferability across chemical families unclear.

model_io.model: QM/MM molecular system with multipole expansion

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multipole expansion enables accurate long-range interaction modeling in ML potentials across electronic states
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via excellent prediction accuracy and nonadiabatic dynamics simulations

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45038-7.task: Training parametric interactions in analog bosonic quantum neural networks via Fock-basis measurement
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45038-7.key_results: Hybrid training scheme combining efficient classical Heisenberg-picture simulation with experimental quantum device implementation; nonlinear features extracted via Fock-basis measurements from linear Gaussian dynamics; compatible with circuit QED and integrated photonic platforms; gradient-based optimization enabled without on-device measurement
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45038-7.comments: Clever exploitation of linear-dynamics nonlinear-measurement duality enables practical training; scalability to deep networks and learning capacity bounds relative to classical NNs remain underexplored.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Linear bosonic networks with nonlinear Fock measurement enable trainable quantum neural networks via classical gradient simulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via demonstration of hybrid training framework

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45446-9.task: Designing multiband THz MIMO antenna with metamaterial integration for biomedical/security applications
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45446-9.key_results: Operating frequencies 3.8 and 6.4 THz; simulated gain 9.2/8.9 dBi; isolation −43/−37 dB; MIMO diversity gain 9.2/9.5 dB; compact 38×38×100 μm³ design
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45446-9.comments: Strong antenna metrics achieved but simulation-only validation; practical fabrication at submicron scale and experimental verification of gain/isolation needed.

model_io.model: Symmetric stub-loaded microstrip MIMO antenna with butterfly metamaterial

hypotheses.hypothesis: Metamaterial integration enhances gain and bandwidth simultaneously
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via simulations showing 9+ dBi gain at dual frequency bands

hf:2603.22918.key_results: 6-12% improvement over MLLM baselines, 1-3% gain over prior adaptive methods on six video benchmarks
hf:2603.22918.comments: Three-stage pipeline (SFT→KTO→GRPO) is novel but adds complexity; unclear how much improvement comes from each stage versus the planning-first strategy.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Planning-before-perception enables more efficient video understanding than perception-first strategies
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via 6-12% improvement over perception-first baselines

hf:2603.22341.task: Red-teaming LLM agents via trajectory-aware evolutionary search
hf:2603.22341.key_results: Substantially higher attack realization rate (ARR) than baselines; effective against GPT-5.2, Gemini-3-Pro, Qwen3.5, GLM-5
hf:2603.22341.comments: First work to focus on multi-step tool execution vulnerabilities in MCP; methodology is sound but exact ARR numbers not disclosed in abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Trajectory-aware search better discovers agent vulnerabilities than prior text-only red-teaming
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; T-MAP outperforms baselines in ARR across multiple frontier models

hf:2603.24533.task: Mobile GUI automation via self-evolving agent with failed experience learning
hf:2603.24533.key_results: 4B model achieves 81.0% Pass@1 success rate on AndroidWorld, exceeding human performance
hf:2603.24533.comments: GRSD's identification of fork points is clever but requires group rollouts, adding computational cost; strong empirical results warrant attention.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-evolution via failed trajectory correction outperforms sparse reward learning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; RFT+GRSD achieves 81% success, surpassing baselines and human performance

hf:2603.24472.task: Understanding self-distillation degradation in mathematical reasoning
hf:2603.24472.key_results: Performance drops up to 40% in mathematical reasoning; suppression of epistemic verbalization identified as root cause
hf:2603.24472.comments: Well-designed controlled experiments; finding is important but applies primarily to reasoning tasks—effectiveness may differ for other domains.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Conditioning teacher on rich context suppresses uncertainty, harming OOD reasoning performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; degradation up to 40% observed; uncertainty expression crucial for robust reasoning

hf:2603.24329.task: Multi-video understanding and reasoning for autonomous agents in 3D environments
hf:2603.24329.key_results: Benchmarks MLLMs with 2.4K diagnostic QA pairs at 1.22 labels/second; reveals substantial gap from human performance
hf:2603.24329.comments: GameplayQA's dense annotation and triadic decomposition (Self/Other/World) are valuable; benchmark is well-structured but limited to gameplay settings.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current MLLMs struggle with temporal grounding, agent attribution, and decision density in multi-agent environments
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; common failure modes identified through diagnostic QA evaluation

hf:2603.21289.task: Unsupervised self-evolution for multimodal reasoning without human annotations
hf:2603.21289.key_results: Consistent improvements on five mathematical reasoning benchmarks using only self-consistency signal and GRPO
hf:2603.21289.comments: Novel use of group-relative scores to convert absolute to relative advantages is elegant; scalability claims strong but limited to mathematical domains.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-consistency signal enables stable self-evolution without external reward models or human annotations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; group relative policy optimization achieves improvements without supervision

hf:2603.24157.task: Long-horizon computer task automation in healthcare systems
hf:2603.24157.key_results: CarePilot outperforms closed-source/open-source baselines by ~15.26% and 3.38% on CareFlow benchmark
hf:2603.24157.comments: Actor-critic with dual memory is well-motivated; CareFlow benchmark valuable for healthcare domain but limited scope (4 application types).

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dual-memory (long-term + short-term) with iterative refinement improves long-horizon medical workflow automation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CarePilot achieves SOTA on healthcare task benchmark

hf:2603.21618.task: 360° dynamic object reconstruction from monocular video
hf:2603.21618.key_results: SOTA on iPhone360, iPhone, DAVIS datasets; robust to 135° separation between training and test views
hf:2603.21618.comments: 3D-native initialization via AnchorTAP3D is principled; iPhone360 benchmark is useful but relatively small scale.

hypotheses.hypothesis: 3D-native initialization mitigates 2D-native prior overfitting and improves occluded region consistency
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SOTA results on 360° reconstruction with large view separation

hf:2603.24440.task: Providing large-scale video demonstrations for computer-use agents
hf:2603.24440.key_results: VideoCUA: ~10K tasks, 55 hours continuous video, 6M frames; GroundCUA: 56K screenshots, 3.6M UI annotations; current models ~60% failure rate
hf:2603.24440.comments: CUA-Suite is a major resource contribution; 55 hours of continuous video is transformative for the field but still modest compared to internet scale.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Continuous video with temporal dynamics is critical for scaling computer-use agents beyond sparse screenshots
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via dataset design; high failure rate on professional apps suggests this is a key bottleneck

hf:2603.24506.task: Physically consistent driving video generation under challenging trajectories
hf:2603.24506.key_results: PhyGenesis outperforms SOTA especially on challenging trajectories; trained with physics-rich heterogeneous dataset (real + CARLA)
hf:2603.24506.comments: Physical condition generator and physics-enhanced decoder are well-motivated; CARLA simulation augmentation is clever but may introduce domain bias.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Learning from challenging trajectories in simulation improves physical consistency under counterfactual conditions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CARLA-augmented training enables trajectory correction and consistent generation

hf:2603.24458.task: Unified omni-level video generation with multimodal composition and reasoning
hf:2603.24458.key_results: OmniWeaving SOTA among open-source unified models; handles diverse compositional and reasoning-augmented scenarios
hf:2603.24458.comments: Ambitious scope (text, multi-image, video inputs) but IntelligentVBench is newly proposed—independent validation needed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reasoning-informed multimodal composition enables sophisticated unified video generation
hypotheses.result: Claimed; SOTA on newly proposed IntelligentVBench but limited external validation

hf:2603.23638.task: Evaluating LLM agents on long-horizon resource allocation under uncertainty
hf:2603.23638.key_results: EnterpriseArena benchmark with 132-month simulator; only 16% of runs survive full horizon; larger models don't reliably outperform smaller
hf:2603.23638.comments: Excellent benchmark design with partial observability and budgeted tools; finding that scale doesn't guarantee performance is valuable.

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLM agents struggle with long-horizon resource allocation under uncertainty, a distinct capability gap
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 84% failure rate and poor scaling with model size

hf:2603.23404.task: 3D spatial reasoning in multimodal models via textual representation
hf:2603.23404.key_results: TRACE prompting yields consistent improvements on VSI-Bench and OST-Bench across diverse MLLM backbones
hf:2603.23404.comments: Cognitive theory motivation (allocentric reasoning) is well-grounded; TRACE is a prompting method rather than architectural change, limiting impact.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Intermediate text-based 3D representations improve spatial reasoning in egocentric video
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; TRACE yields consistent improvements across MLLM scales and architectures

hf:2603.22120.task: Unified streaming video understanding and embodied intelligence
hf:2603.22120.key_results: StreamingClaw integrates real-time reasoning, multimodal memory, and closed-loop perception-decision-action; OpenClaw-compatible
hf:2603.22120.comments: Ambitious framework combining five core capabilities; technical report format limits quantitative validation—benchmarks unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unified streaming architecture enables proactive embodied intelligence with long-term memory
hypotheses.result: Not quantitatively validated in abstract; design principles sound but evaluation lacking

hf:2603.18742.task: Inference-time mixed-precision quantization for video diffusion models
hf:2603.18742.key_results: 1.92× end-to-end acceleration, 3.32× memory reduction; NVFP4/INT8 mixed-precision with temporal delta cache
hf:2603.18742.comments: Linear correlation between input-output difference and quantization sensitivity is insightful; temporal delta cache exploits redundancy well.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive precision allocation based on block sensitivity and temporal redundancy enables aggressive quantization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 1.92× speedup and 3.32× memory reduction without quality loss

hf:2603.23478.key_results: UniFunc3D achieves 59.9% mIoU improvement on SceneFun3D, surpassing training-free and training-based methods; training-free
hf:2603.23478.comments: Active spatial-temporal grounding with coarse-to-fine strategy is elegant; impressive gains but limited to single benchmark.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Joint spatial-temporal reasoning with adaptive frame selection enables fine-grained functionality grounding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 59.9% mIoU improvement with training-free approach

hf:2603.20176.task: Real-time novel view synthesis with latent 3D features
hf:2603.20176.key_results: SOTA deterministic NVS (31.4 PSNR on Re10k), real-time inference, generalizes to in-the-wild data
hf:2603.20176.comments: 3D-aware latent features pre-trained on explicit 3D supervision is sound; lightweight decoder enables real-time performance.

hypotheses.hypothesis: 3D-aware latent features from pre-trained 3D supervision improve feed-forward NVS performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SOTA PSNR and real-time inference on multiple benchmarks

hf:2603.23539.task: Understanding reasoning in LLMs via self-organized criticality
hf:2603.23539.key_results: LLMs trained at criticality exhibit reasoning; order parameter derived from model weights predicts reasoning capability without benchmark evaluation
hf:2603.23539.comments: Novel theoretical framework inspired by physics phase transitions; claims are bold but validation relies on existing benchmarks, not independent testing.

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLMs trained at self-organized criticality exhibit reasoning behavior analogous to phase transitions
hypotheses.result: Partially supported; benchmark scores correlate with criticality but mechanism unproven

nature:10.1038/s41388-026-03739-3.task: Establish NPC1-USP7-p53 axis linking cholesterol metabolism to HCC proliferation
nature:10.1038/s41388-026-03739-3.key_results: NPC1 upregulated in HCC tissues and associated with poor prognosis; NPC1 silencing suppresses proliferation in vitro and in vivo; NPC1 disrupts USP7-p53 binding, enhancing p53 degradation; NPC1 modulates cholesterol via p53-SREBP2 axis
nature:10.1038/s41388-026-03739-3.comments: Elegant dual-mechanism model linking lysosomal cholesterol transport, p53 degradation, and HCC growth; identifies actionable therapeutic vulnerabilities.

hypotheses.hypothesis: NPC1 promotes HCC by disrupting USP7-p53 interaction
hypotheses.result: NPC1 enhances p53 ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation

hypotheses.hypothesis: NPC1 modulates cholesterol metabolism in p53-dependent manner
hypotheses.result: NPC1-mediated cholesterol reduction reversed by p53 knockdown

nature:10.1038/s41389-026-00608-2.task: Review oncogenic control of nucleotide synthesis in cancer
nature:10.1038/s41389-026-00608-2.key_results: TP53 loss, LKB1 loss, PI3K-AKT-mTORC1, MYC, and RAS coordinate nucleotide biosynthesis; integration of glycolysis, pentose-phosphate pathway, folate-dependent one-carbon metabolism, and glutamine/aspartate provisioning
nature:10.1038/s41389-026-00608-2.comments: Comprehensive mechanistic review organizing complex metabolic reprogramming into actionable therapeutic model; nucleotide metabolism represents central nexus of oncogenic signaling.

inputs: oncogenic lesions (TP53 loss, LKB1 loss, PI3K-AKT-mTORC1, MYC, RAS)
outputs: de novo purine and pyrimidine synthesis rates

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tumors selectively activate de novo versus salvage nucleotide synthesis based on environmental context
hypotheses.result: Environment-shaped routing model explains metabolic plasticity and therapy response

nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08626-z.task: Identify cytosolic ACAD9 function in suppressing osteoclast differentiation
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08626-z.key_results: ACAD9 blocks TRAF6-mediated K63-polyubiquitination and promotes K48-linked degradation; Acad9 knockout mice show increased osteoclast numbers and decreased bone mass
nature:10.1038/s41419-026-08626-z.comments: Reveals moonlighting function of a mitochondrial protein in cytosolic signaling, with clear therapeutic relevance to osteoporosis.

hypotheses.hypothesis: ACAD9 suppresses TRAF6 signaling by competitive binding
hypotheses.result: ACAD9 prevents UBC13/UEV1A interaction with TRAF6

hypotheses.hypothesis: ACAD9 degrades TRAF6 via proteasomal pathway
hypotheses.result: K48-linked polyubiquitination and proteasomal degradation confirmed

nature:10.1038/s41420-026-03071-5.task: Elucidate polystyrene nanoplastic promotion of endometrial cancer via ACSS2-arachidonic acid metabolism
nature:10.1038/s41420-026-03071-5.key_results: PS-NPs accumulate in endometrial cells and organoids; AMPK activates ACSS2 and promotes nuclear translocation; nuclear ACSS2 increases H3K9 acetylation and PLA2G3 expression; elevated arachidonic acid drives EMT
nature:10.1038/s41420-026-03071-5.comments: Novel environmental health mechanism linking nanoplastic exposure to cancer progression; ACSS2 emerges as key metabolic-epigenetic node but clinical relevance requires validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: PS-NPs activate AMPK-ACSS2-PLA2G3 axis to promote EMT
hypotheses.result: ACSS2 nuclear translocation increases H3K9 acetylation and arachidonic acid production

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-71052-4.task: Identify conserved morphodynamics but divergent Ca²⁺ control in cell extravasation
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-71052-4.key_results: All extravasating cells form Ca²⁺-dependent membrane blebs; PGCs and HT-1080 use store-operated Ca²⁺ entry (SOCE); epithelial-derived PC-3 and MDA-MB-231 use IP₃R-mediated ER release
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-71052-4.comments: Reveals convergent phenotype (blebbing) driven by divergent molecular mechanisms; evolutionary mapping to ancestral and metazoan pathways provides conceptual framework.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Bleb-based extravasation is morphodynamically conserved but uses distinct Ca²⁺ pathways
hypotheses.result: PGCs/HT-1080 use SOCE; epithelial-derived cancers use IP₃R pathway

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-71105-8.task: Map genome-wide c-di-GMP regulation using FRET biosensors and cell sorting
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-71105-8.key_results: Large toolkit of FRET biosensors with diverse binding affinities; FRET-To-Sort identified flagellar genes affecting c-di-GMP levels; inhibited flagellar rotation reduces c-di-GMP biosynthesis via proton motive force
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-71105-8.comments: Novel methodological approach combining FRET with transposon screening; connection between motility and signaling metabolism provides mechanistic insight.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Flagellar rotation modulates c-di-GMP biosynthesis
hypotheses.result: Inhibited flagellar rotation increases proton motive force and reduces c-di-GMP

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-71108-5.task: Establish KAT8 K145 lactylation as regulator of p53 pro-apoptotic function and doxorubicin cardiotoxicity
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-71108-5.key_results: GCN5 catalyzes KAT8 K145 lactylation; SIRT6 delactylates; K145 lactylation promotes KAT8-TIP60 complex formation and p53 K120 acetylation; doxorubicin increases K145 lactylation; glimepiride mitigates doxorubicin-induced cardiomyocyte apoptosis
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-71108-5.comments: Links emerging lactylation PTM to p53 activation and defines mechanism of anthracycline cardiotoxicity; glimepiride repurposing offers translational opportunity.

hypotheses.hypothesis: KAT8 K145 lactylation drives p53 pro-apoptotic function
hypotheses.result: K145 lactylation enhances KAT8-TIP60 complex and p53 K120 acetylation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Glimepiride inhibits KAT8 lactylation and mitigates doxorubicin cardiotoxicity
hypotheses.result: Glimepiride disrupts GCN5-KAT8 interaction and reduces p53 activation in cardiomyocytes

nature:10.1038/s41583-026-01032-6.task: Review dysregulated astrocyte Ca²⁺ homeostasis in neurodegenerative disease pathophysiology
nature:10.1038/s41583-026-01032-6.key_results: Astrocyte Ca²⁺ signaling progressively remodeled during aging and dysregulated in Alzheimer, Parkinson, Huntington, and ALS; alterations heterogeneous and context-dependent; Ca²⁺ dysregulation compromises homeostatic functions and promotes neuronal vulnerability
nature:10.1038/s41583-026-01032-6.comments: Comprehensive mechanistic review establishing astrocytic Ca²⁺ as central node in neurodegeneration; identifies broad therapeutic opportunity but requires cell-type-specific targeting strategies.

model_io.model: Astrocyte Ca²⁺ signaling in health and disease

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dysregulated astrocyte Ca²⁺ signaling drives neuronal circuit dysfunction
hypotheses.result: Ca²⁺ alterations compromise homeostatic functions and increase neuronal vulnerability

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-42800-9.task: Determine dysferlin role in preserving sarcolemmal nanodomains after myocardial infarction
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-42800-9.key_results: Dysferlin upregulated 230% in WT MI border zone; dysferlin-KO mice show larger infarcts and reduced systolic function; dysferlin clusters at TAT structures and connexin-43 plaques
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-42800-9.comments: Integrates proteomics with super-resolution microscopy to reveal protein stabilization function; dysferlin emerges as therapeutic target for post-MI cardiomyocyte protection.

model_io.model: Left anterior descending artery ligation in mice

inputs: cardiac tissue from infarct, border, remote zones

outputs: dysferlin interaction partners at TAT and ICD

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dysferlin stabilizes sarcolemmal nanodomains in MI border zone
hypotheses.result: Dysferlin clusters at TAT and ICD, protecting membrane architecture

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43742-y.task: Identify driver of gastric chief cell differentiation independent of galectin-8
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43742-y.key_results: Lgals8–/– phenotype (delayed differentiation) attributed to deletions of Mmrn1 and Snca, not Lgals8; Mmrn1–/–/Snca–/– mice show hyperproliferative corpus with elevated nuclear β-catenin; multimerin-1 produced by endothelial cells tempers WNT signaling
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43742-y.comments: Reveals mesodermal-epithelial cross-talk in gastric development; unexpected finding that key phenotype arose from genetic background highlights importance of pure knockout validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multimerin-1 from endothelial cells suppresses WNT signaling in gastric epithelium
hypotheses.result: Mmrn1–/–/Snca–/– mice show elevated β-catenin and hyperproliferation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Galectin-8 plays no role in chief cell homeostasis
hypotheses.result: Pure Lgals8 knockout needed to test; phenotype was due to linked deletions

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44905-7.task: Model MET dynamics and constraints using logical network of EMT/MET inducers
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44905-7.key_results: MET is highly dependent on mesenchymal context; EMT program persistence constrains MET inducers; perturbing one EMT pathway reinforces alternatives, maintaining hybrid states
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44905-7.comments: Systems-level perspective reveals MET as context-dependent and constrained by EMT program redundancy; highlights importance of hybrid epithelial-mesenchymal states in cancer biology.

hypotheses.hypothesis: MET induction depends on mesenchymal context
hypotheses.result: In silico simulations show context-dependent MET trajectories along epithelial-mesenchymal spectrum

hypotheses.hypothesis: EMT program redundancy prevents full MET reversion
hypotheses.result: Perturbing one EMT pathway activates alternative pathways, maintaining hybrid states

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45215-8.task: Characterize ultrastructural and histochemical changes in axolotl lungs during metamorphosis
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45215-8.key_results: Metamorphic lungs show expanded air spaces, dense elastic fibers, and mature lamellar bodies; neotenic lungs show thick alveolar walls and prominent collagen deposition
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45215-8.comments: Provides morphological framework for vertebrate lung adaptation but notes axolotl histology differs from mammalian; relevance to human pulmonary regeneration requires further validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Thyroid hormone induces lung restructuring for terrestrial adaptation
hypotheses.result: Metamorphic lungs show expanded air spaces and mature pneumocyte ultrastructure

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45606-x.task: Evaluate rapamycin efficacy in reducing tendon fibrosis and promoting Achilles tendon healing
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45606-x.key_results: Rapamycin decreased peritendinous fibrosis and modulated microRNA levels; did not enhance intratendinous healing or lesion repair within 3 weeks post-injury
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45606-x.comments: Negative result appropriately documented; rapamycin shows partial benefit (reduced fibrosis) but insufficient for clinical therapeutic efficacy in acute tendon injury model.

model_io.model: Needle-induced Achilles tendon injury in rats

hypotheses.hypothesis: mTOR inhibition enhances tendon healing via modulation of resident cell populations
hypotheses.result: Rapamycin reduced peritendinous fibrosis but did not enhance core tendon healing

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45832-3.task: Assess phytochemical composition, drug-likeness, and bioactivity of Punica granatum seed extract
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45832-3.key_results: Major constituents: 2-oxatricyclo[4.3.1.0(3,8)]decane, 2-heptenal, gallic acid, ellagic acid; anti-genotoxicity 53.31–74.48%; anti-proliferation 15.6%; α-glucosidase inhibition 30.2–61.3%; AChE inhibition up to 67.8%; logP <5 for all compounds (Lipinski compliance)
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45832-3.comments: Comprehensive phytochemical-bioactivity-pharmacokinetics correlation; in vitro activities and favorable drug-like properties suggest potential utility, though in vivo validation lacking.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gallic acid and ellagic acid phenolic derivatives mediate bioactivity
hypotheses.result: Major phenolic constituents show favorable drug-likeness and docking interactions

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45939-7.task: Compare MSC and exosome treatments for endometrial regeneration in intrauterine adhesions
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45939-7.key_results: All treated groups reduced collagen deposition vs positive controls; Adipose-Exo group showed most favorable remodeling scores; IV and local UC-Exo delivery produced comparable effects
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45939-7.comments: Direct comparison of cell versus acellular therapies and delivery routes is valuable, though histological improvements do not guarantee functional restoration of fertility.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Exosomes are as effective as MSCs for endometrial regeneration
hypotheses.result: Exosome groups showed comparable or superior remodeling to MSC groups

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tissue source influences regeneration profile
hypotheses.result: Adipose-Exo group most favorable; UC and adipose sources differ in efficacy

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-71035-5.task: Deep learning phenotypic prediction of complex traits in crops using GWAS-weighted embeddings
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-71035-5.key_results: 77.5% improvement in prediction accuracy vs. baselines, 78% MSE reduction, 1.8-2.4× computational efficiency gain
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-71035-5.comments: Weighted embedding mechanism incorporates GWAS priors effectively; biological interpretability through variant attribution is valuable for crop breeding.

hypotheses.hypothesis: GWAS-derived SNP weighting + Transformer multi-head attention capture long-range genomic dependencies
hypotheses.result: Outperformed 7 baseline models across 6 datasets with 77.5% accuracy improvement

nature:10.1038/s41525-026-00561-4.task: Comprehensive structural variant detection combining optical genome mapping and long-read sequencing
nature:10.1038/s41525-026-00561-4.key_results: Undetected SVs revealed in 14/30 cases (46.7%); gene disruptions explaining clinical features in 7/30 (23.3%)
nature:10.1038/s41525-026-00561-4.comments: Integration of orthogonal methods substantially improves diagnostic resolution; adaptive sampling strategy effectively targets complex breakpoints.

model_io.model: Optical genome mapping (OGM) with ultra-high MW DNA labeling
Oxford Nanopore long-read sequencing with adaptive sampling

hypotheses.hypothesis: OGM detects SVs missed by exome-seq alone
hypotheses.result: 46.7% of cases revealed previously undetected SVs

hypotheses.hypothesis: Combining OGM + long-read sequencing resolves complex multi-segment SVs
hypotheses.result: Complex SVs with numerous breakpoints resolved efficiently

nature:10.1038/s41597-026-07108-3.task: Comprehensive single-cell transcriptomic atlas of gastric and colorectal tissues
nature:10.1038/s41597-026-07108-3.key_results: 574,532 stomach cells and 479,629 colorectal cells across 70 cell types each, 229 stomach and 220 colorectal tissue samples
nature:10.1038/s41597-026-07108-3.comments: Resource paper providing curated reference atlas; utility depends on downstream analytical studies leveraging this foundation.

model_io.model: Single-cell RNA-seq workflow with computational validation

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-37775-6.task: Fractional-order SITR epidemic modeling with deep neural network surrogate
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-37775-6.key_results: BR-DNN solutions in high agreement with Adams-Bashforth-Moulton numerical solutions; validated via MSE, regression analysis, absolute error plots
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-37775-6.comments: Fractional-order model captures memory-dependent disease dynamics; BR-DNN provides efficient computational alternative with independent validation.

model_io.model: Fractional-order SITR with Caputo derivative and saturated incidence
outputs: Susceptible, Infectious, Treated, Recovered populations over time

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fractional-order (α < 1) captures long-term memory effects absent in integer-order models
hypotheses.result: Numerical experiments demonstrated importance of memory effects on disease transmission/treatment dynamics

hypotheses.hypothesis: BR-DNN provides accurate data-driven surrogate for fractional ODE solutions
hypotheses.result: High agreement between BR-DNN and Adams-Bashforth-Moulton solutions

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-42330-4.key_results: Compounds 2c, 2d, 2b showed binding affinities -9.312, -9.303, -9.269 kcal/mol (vs ATP -8.46); 2d optimal MM-PBSA ΔG_bind -34.50 ± 0.42 kcal/mol
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-42330-4.comments: Computational predictions require experimental validation; MM-PBSA estimates are sensitive to force field and solvation assumptions.

hypotheses.hypothesis: 2-thiohydantoin derivatives with narrow HOMO-LUMO gaps are reactive CDK2 inhibitors
hypotheses.result: Compounds 2b-e showed 3.02–3.26 eV gaps and superior binding affinity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Key electrophilic centers C2, O12, C14 mediate protein interactions
hypotheses.result: QTAIM and Fukui analyses confirmed these sites; docking showed critical H-bonds with Lys33, Thr14

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43579-5.task: High-throughput automated viability monitoring of C. elegans
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43579-5.key_results: 95% live-count accuracy, 0.999 Pearson correlation with manual counts over 30 days, 4-5× throughput improvement
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-43579-5.comments: Hardware-software integration addresses real practical bottlenecks in aging research, though validation limited to adult worms.

model_io.model: Integrated imaging system (collimated illumination + stepper motor + industrial cameras)

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44194-0.task: Temperature-dependent gating mechanisms in TRPV3 ion channel
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44194-0.key_results: Symmetric cold/heat activation below 30°C via Gibbs-Helmholtz equation; inactivation blocked above 30°C when lipid released by THCV; tetramer-to-pentamer transition during inactivation
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44194-0.comments: Heat capacity model validated for TRPV3; inactivation pathway reveals unexpected quaternary structure dynamics and lipid-dependent gating.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cold and heat activation of TRPV3 are symmetric and share a single thermosensitivity mechanism
hypotheses.result: Initial cold and heat-evoked open states showed similar thermosensitivity despite distinct structures; heat capacity model supported

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lipid binding at vanilloid site gates TRPV3 activation/inactivation below 30°C
hypotheses.result: THCV-induced inactivation only below 30°C; no inactivation above 30°C when lipid released

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44264-3.task: Machine learning classification of ACL injury status from gait spatiotemporal features
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44264-3.key_results: KNN achieved 0.98 balanced accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score; AUC 0.99; stride/stance/swing time and A-P velocity most predictive
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44264-3.comments: Strong gait-based biomarkers identified; temporal features outperform spatial, aligning with known post-ACL kinematic changes.

model_io.model: KNN, GBM, XGBoost, RF, SVM, MLP classifiers

hypotheses.hypothesis: Post-ACL reconstruction gait changes are detectable by ML from treadmill kinematics
hypotheses.result: KNN identified subtle differences with 0.98 balanced accuracy; temporal gait variables most critical

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44665-4.task: Histone PTM profiling to identify epigenetic drug response signatures in T-ALL
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44665-4.key_results: Distinct hPTM signatures linked to sensitivity for each drug class (HDACi, DNMTi, anthracyclines); in vitro signatures poorly predict in vivo (PDX) responses
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-44665-4.comments: MS-based PTM profiling is sensitive; substantial in vitro-in vivo discrepancy highlights model selection importance for biomarker development.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Baseline histone PTM profiles predict in vitro drug sensitivity
hypotheses.result: Distinct signatures identified for each drug class; substantial in vitro-in vivo discrepancies observed

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45458-5.task: Systematic evaluation of pre-trained protein embeddings for AAV vector viability prediction
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45458-5.key_results: Amino acid-level embeddings outperform sequence-level in supervised tasks before fine-tuning; sequence-level optimal after fine-tuning; task-specific fine-tuning essential for sparse mutations
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45458-5.comments: Fine-tuning critically improves performance; dense embeddings require extent of sequence variation exceeding typical bioengineering studies.

model_io.model: ProtBERT and ESM2 embedding variants (amino acid and sequence-level)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Amino acid-level representations capture localized sequence signals better than global sequence embeddings
hypotheses.result: Amino acid embeddings outperformed sequence-level in supervised tasks before fine-tuning

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fine-tuning with task-specific labels compensates for limited bioengineering sequence variation
hypotheses.result: Optimal performance achieved with fine-tuned sequence-level embeddings despite sparse mutations

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45572-4.task: Multi-epitope vaccine design against Ruminococcus torques
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45572-4.key_results: GC content 49.29%, CAI 0.875 indicating high expression potential; vaccine-TLR4 complex stable by MD simulation
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45572-4.comments: In silico design lacks experimental immunogenicity and protective efficacy validation; clinical translation requires wet-lab confirmation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: FtsX, single-stranded DNA binding protein, and FtsW are strong immunogenic targets
hypotheses.result: Identified as final target candidates by proteomics screening

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-epitope vaccine with TLR4 adjuvant induces stable immune response
hypotheses.result: MD simulations confirmed vaccine-TLR4 complex stability; requires experimental validation

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45682-z.task: Lightweight deep learning architecture for logo classification
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45682-z.key_results: Near-saturation accuracy on FlickrLogos-32, BelgaLogos, WebLogo-2M benchmarks; low computational overhead maintained
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45682-z.comments: Benchmark saturation limits interpretability of performance claims; real-world generalization to novel logos remains unclear.

model_io.model: LogoXpertNet (MobileNetV3 + SE blocks + CLFF + HSE-SAB + FA-CBAM)

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45879-2.task: Automated medical documentation and clinical decision support system
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45879-2.key_results: All tested components achieved >80% effectiveness threshold; applicable to internal medicine and pediatrics
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45879-2.comments: Functional tests demonstrate high performance; clinical impact and user adoption rates not detailed.

nature:10.1038/s41746-026-02540-6.task: Deep learning-based spine curvature phenotyping from DXA imaging for scoliosis GWAS
nature:10.1038/s41746-026-02540-6.key_results: Automated curvature correlated 0.83 with clinical Cobb angle; GWAS identified 2 novel loci (SEM1/SHFM1, chr3 lncRNA); outperformed case-control GWAS with 10× larger sample
nature:10.1038/s41746-026-02540-6.comments: Quantitative imaging phenotypes capture genetic signal missed by categorical case-control labels; validates precision phenotyping for genomic discovery.

model_io.model: Deep learning vertebral segmentation + landmarking (U-Net or SegNet)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quantitative imaging phenotypes identify more genome-wide significant loci than categorical case-control datasets
hypotheses.result: Identified 2 novel loci vs. case-control study with 10× sample size

hypotheses.hypothesis: Novel scoliosis loci in SEM1/SHFM1 and chr3 lncRNA (EDEM1-GRM7) are functionally relevant
hypotheses.result: Loci identified by quantitative GWAS; functional mechanisms require downstream investigation

nature:10.1038/s44220-026-00593-w.task: Computational model of craving-learning interaction in substance-related decision-making
nature:10.1038/s44220-026-00593-w.key_results: Momentary craving biased substance-specific learning rate (opposite directions in alcohol vs. cannabis); model-derived parameters predicted alcohol, not cannabis, addiction risk
nature:10.1038/s44220-026-00593-w.comments: Computational approach disentangles craving-choice coupling mechanisms; selective predictive utility suggests domain-specific clinical utility.

model_io.model: Reinforcement learning task (addictive cues vs. money) + craving questionnaires
Predictive regression (model parameters → addiction risk scores)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Momentary craving and reinforcement learning bi-directionally influence each other
hypotheses.result: Craving biased learning rate (opposite directions by substance); outcomes influenced craving

hypotheses.hypothesis: Model-derived parameters improve addiction risk prediction vs. baseline measures
hypotheses.result: Parameters predicted alcohol, but not cannabis, addiction risk scores

nature:10.1038/s44259-026-00192-w.task: AI framework for early detection and risk prediction of antimicrobial resistance in aquatic ecosystems
nature:10.1038/s44259-026-00192-w.key_results: Integrated omics, environmental, and hydrological data into spatiotemporal predictive models for real-time AMR monitoring
nature:10.1038/s44259-026-00192-w.comments: Vision paper outlining potential of One Health AMR surveillance; requires implementation of governance, ethics, and capacity-building frameworks.

nature:10.1038/s44433-026-00004-x.task: Video-based behavior detection and tracking of individual cattle for dairy digital twins

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45131-x.key_results: REDA outperforms 13 comparators on 29 CEC2017 benchmarks and 19 constrained engineering problems; Friedman/Wilcoxon statistical significance
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45131-x.comments: Solid metaheuristic contribution with rigorous validation; low-dimensional advantage is noted; solar parameter estimation application is practical.

model_io.model: REDA: adaptive search weight cycling + stochastic recombination + elite memory
inputs: black-box optimization problems (29 CEC2017 + 19 constrained engineering)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive cyclic search weight balances exploration-exploitation better than fixed strategies
hypotheses.result: REDA significantly outperforms 13 state-of-the-art algorithms; especially strong in low-dimensional spaces

nature:10.1038/s42005-026-02592-w.key_results: General distance measure for weighted hypergraphs reduces to classic graph distance; applied to arXiv preprint network with cognitive distance weighting
nature:10.1038/s42005-026-02592-w.comments: Theoretical contribution addressing a genuine gap; cognitive distance weighting is well-motivated; real-world applicability demonstrated but limited.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hypergraph distance measure avoids information loss from clique projection
hypotheses.result: Full hypergraph measure necessary for real-world datasets; clique projection loses significant structural information

nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70948-5.task: Enable damage-free transfer of soft electronics onto curved and fragile biological surfaces
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70948-5.key_results: Yield-stress fluid at 0.0253 kPa (orders below tissue rupture ~0.2 kPa); demonstrated thermal sensing on moving joints without device or tissue damage
nature:10.1038/s41467-026-70948-5.comments: Innovative fluid mechanics solution with elegant biological application; biocompatibility and long-term stability remain to be established.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ultra-low yield stress and adhesion-switching enable non-invasive transfer without deformation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; successful thermal sensing on moving joints with preserved device and tissue integrity

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45779-5.task: Demonstrate poling-induced second-order optical nonlinearity in amorphous sodium niobate waveguides
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45779-5.key_results: Sustained second-order nonlinearity after waveguide processing (UV lithography, dry-etch); precise spatial control of SONL via micrometer-scale poling
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45779-5.comments: Attractive alternative to lithium niobate with isotropic design advantages, but efficiency and scalability for practical devices require further optimization.

model_io.model: Amorphous sodium niobate thin-film waveguides with thermal poling

hypotheses.hypothesis: Thermal poling induces persistent SONL in amorphous Na-niobate, retained through fabrication
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; strong SHG signals persist after UV lithography and dry-etching

nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45929-9.task: Characterize electron temperature and density in RF-DBD Ar/CH₄ plasma across operational parameter ranges
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45929-9.key_results: Electron temperature 0.98–1.08 eV (Ar, 0.5–1.0 Torr), ~1.2 eV (Ar/CH₄) at 4 W; electron density 10¹⁴–10¹⁵ m⁻³; Hα intensity correlates with hydrogen species; deposited carbon films amorphous structure
nature:10.1038/s41598-026-45929-9.comments: Systematic parametric characterization useful for plasma process control; connection to hydrogen production efficiency and carbon material quality could be strengthened.

model_io.model: RF-DBD Ar/CH₄ plasma with optical diagnostics

hypotheses.hypothesis: Plasma operational parameters systematically control electron temperature, density, and gas conversion efficiency
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; pressure, power, and flow rate relationships quantified for controlled H₂ production
